| D50 Malicious Crate SSH Authorized-Key Persistence + Telegram Session Exfiltration | malicious crate modifies `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`, steals `.env` / credential-like JSONs / docs, and exfiltrates Telegram Desktop `tdata` for durable re-entry | long-lived workstation/server persistence, operator-session hijack, repo/CI/keeper lateral movement even after simple key rotation | current `Cargo.lock` is clean, so **NOT ACTIVE today**; but keeper `.env` + Solana keypair paths make the blast radius high if any builder/operator host ingests such a crate |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-18 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D50** added after reclassifying the `microsoftsystem64` cluster as a distinct persistence-and-session-hijack pattern rather than a mere reinforcement of earlier typosquat families. Microstable has **no new active CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM on-chain finding** in this cycle, but operator/build-host hardening requirements expanded.

## 2026-04-19 Toolchain Determinism Boundary Pattern Addition

### D51. Anchor JS Lockfile Drift / Semver-Satisfying Supply-Chain Smuggle

**Source signals (2026-04-19 sweep)**:
- Anchor commit `4b8f0e0` (`fix: enforce --frozen-lockfile for yarn install calls (#4228)`, 2026-04-16)
- supporting context: Anchor recent docs/CLI surface still centers `Anchor.toml` `package_manager = "yarn"`, meaning package-manager invocations remain a first-class part of the developer workflow

**Key insight**: кё°мЎҙ кіөкёүл§қ лІЎн„° лӢӨмҲҳлҠ” "к°ңл°ңмһҗк°Җ мғҲ м•…м„ұ нҢЁнӮӨм§ҖлҘј м§Ғм ‘ м¶”к°Җн•ңлӢӨ"лҠ” м „м ңлҘј л‘”лӢӨ. мқҙлІҲ мӢ нҳёлҠ” лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ. **лҸ„кө¬мІҙмқё мһҗмІҙк°Җ `yarn`/`yarn install` мқ„ lockfile immutability м—Ҷмқҙ нҳём¶ңн•ҳл©ҙ, мқҙлҜё н—Ҳмҡ©лҗң semver range м•ҲмңјлЎң л“Өм–ҙмҳӨлҠ” мғҲ transitive лІ„м „л§ҢмңјлЎңлҸ„ кіөкІ©мқҙ м„ұлҰҪ**н•ңлӢӨ. мҰү кіөкІ©мһҗлҠ” maintainerм—җкІҢ л…ёкіЁм Ғмқё м•…м„ұ dependencyлҘј м¶”к°ҖмӢңнӮӨм§Җ м•Ҡм•„лҸ„, кё°мЎҙ range м•Ҳмқҳ мғҲ лҰҙлҰ¬мҠӨлҘј л…ёлҰ¬л©ҙ лҗңлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. attacker compromises or publishes a malicious JS package version that still satisfies an existing semver range in the Anchor client/tooling dependency graph.
2. maintainer runs `anchor test`, workspace/client scaffolding, or another Anchor-driven command on an older/unhardened toolchain path.
3. Anchor shells out to `yarn` without `--frozen-lockfile`, allowing `yarn.lock` refresh or partial re-resolution.
4. refreshed dependency graph executes `postinstall`, build-time, or runtime side effects inside the developer environment.
5. attacker steals deploy/devnet wallets, `.env`, SSH material, browser/session tokens, or rewrites generated TS client artifacts before review focuses on the actual Solana code change.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D43 / A44 / A45 / D50** = operatorк°Җ мғҲ crate/package лҳҗлҠ” clone кі„м—ҙмқ„ м§Ғм ‘ л“Өм—¬мҳӨлҠ” кІҪлЎңк°Җ мӨ‘мӢ¬мқҙлӢӨ.
- **D51** = repo diffм—җ мғҲ м•…м„ұ dependencyк°Җ м—ҶлҚ”лқјлҸ„, **toolchain non-determinism** н•ҳлӮҳлЎң мқҙлҜё н—Ҳмҡ©лҗң semver лІ”мң„ м•Ҳм—җм„ң кіөкІ© лІ„м „мқҙ мҠӨл©°л“ңлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ.
- **A109** = Anchor lifecycle hooks к°ҷмқҖ лӘ…мӢңм Ғ мӢӨн–ү нӣ… л¬ём ң.
- **D51** = нӣ…мқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **lockfile refresh boundary мһҗмІҙ**к°Җ кіөкІ©л©ҙмқҙ лҗңлӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. `anchor test` / client generation / example buildлҘј "к·ёлғҘ к°ңл°ң нҺёмқҳ лӘ…л №"мңјлЎң м·Ёкёүн•ҳкі , л„ӨнҠёмӣҢнҒ¬ кё°л°ҳ dependency refresh кІҪкі„лЎң ліҙм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
2. lockfile diffлҠ” мӣҗлһҳ noisyн•ҙм„ң workspace version bump, scaffold refresh, docs syncмҷҖ н•Ёк»ҳ мүҪкІҢ л¬»нһҢлӢӨ.
3. Rust on-chain / keeper мҪ”л“ң лҰ¬л·°м—җ л№„н•ҙ JS client/tooling dependency graphлҠ” к°җмӢң к°•лҸ„к°Җ лӮ®лӢӨ.
4. caret rangeмҷҖ semver-compatible updateлҠ” мң м§ҖліҙмҲҳм„ұ мӢ нҳёмІҳлҹј ліҙмқҙм§Җл§Ң, кіөкІ©мһҗм—җкІҢлҠ” review frictionмқҙ лӮ®мқҖ м№ЁнҲ¬лЎңк°Җ лҗңлӢӨ.

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/Anchor.toml` лҠ” `package_manager = "yarn"` мқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ңлӢӨ.
- `microstable/solana/package.json` лҠ” `@coral-xyz/anchor` мҷҖ `@solana/spl-token` мқ„ к°Ғк°Ғ `^0.31.1`, `^0.4.9` лЎң м„ м–ён•ңлӢӨ.
- `yarn.lock` лҠ” мЎҙмһ¬н•ҳм§Җл§Ң, upstream hardening signalмқҖ **Anchor-invoked install pathк°Җ lockfile immutabilityлҘј лі„лҸ„ к°•м ңн•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡмңјл©ҙ мһ¬н•ҙм„қлҗ  мҲҳ мһҲм—ҲлӢӨ**лҠ” м җмқ„ ліҙм—¬мӨҖлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң нҳ„мһ¬ Microstableм—җлҠ” **ACTIVE LATENT** developer/build-path exposureк°Җ мһҲлӢӨ. мқҙлҠ” on-chain bugк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј build integrity riskлӢӨ.

**Defensive heuristic**:
- Anchorк°Җ нҳём¶ңн•ҳлҠ” лӘЁл“  package-manager кІҪлЎңм—җ `--frozen-lockfile` лҳҗлҠ” лҸҷл“ұн•ң immutable-install м •мұ…мқ„ к°•м ңн•  кІғ
- build/test мӨ‘ `yarn.lock` ліҖкІҪмқҙ л°ңмғқн•ҳл©ҙ мҰүмӢң fail мӢңнӮ¬ кІғ
- security-sensitive repoм—җм„ңлҠ” JS dependencyмқҳ caret range мӮ¬мҡ©мқ„ м¶•мҶҢн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ review gateлҘј л‘ҳ кІғ
- Anchor client generation/testлҠ” deploy wallet, SSH key, production `.env` к°Җ м—ҶлҠ” кІ©лҰ¬ нҷҳкІҪм—җм„ң мҲҳн–үн•  кІғ
- dependency refresh м§Ғнӣ„ generated TS client artifact diffлҘј н•„мҲҳ кІҖнҶ  лҢҖмғҒмңјлЎң л‘ҳ кІғ

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D51 Anchor JS Lockfile Drift / Semver-Satisfying Supply-Chain Smuggle | Anchor-driven package-manager call runs without immutable lockfile enforcement, allowing semver-satisfying transitive refresh during routine build/test/scaffold flows | silent developer-environment compromise, generated client artifact tampering, wallet / SSH / `.env` exfiltration without explicit new dependency add | `Anchor.toml` uses `package_manager = "yarn"`, package.json uses caret ranges, and `yarn.lock` exists; **ACTIVE LATENT** until immutable-install discipline is guaranteed end-to-end |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-19 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D51** added from Anchor's upstream `--frozen-lockfile` hardening signal. Microstable has **one new MEDIUM active-latent builder-path finding** in this cycle; no new CRITICAL/HIGH on-chain exploit was confirmed.

## 2026-04-20 Anchor Signer-Downgrade Serialization Pattern Addition

### A117. Anchor Composite AccountMeta Signer-Override Drop / Privilege Downgrade Bypass

**Source signals (2026-04-20 sweep)**:
- `solana-foundation/anchor` commit `55daadb` (`fix: Client is_signer usage in to_account_metas (#3322)`, 2026-04-15)
- upstream regression test in the same patch shows a `proxy` instruction forwarding nested account metas with `.to_account_metas(Some(false))`, where the old generated path failed to clear signer state on composite accounts

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ proxy / adapter / router / keeper helperм—җм„ң мҷёл¶Җ н”„лЎңк·ёлһЁмңјлЎң кі„м •мқ„ л„ҳкёё л•Ң `to_account_metas(Some(false))` к°ҷмқҖ лӘ…мӢңм Ғ overrideлҘј мҚЁм„ң **signer privilegeлҘј мқҳлҸ„м ҒмңјлЎң м ңкұ°н–ҲлӢӨкі  лҜҝлҠ”лӢӨ**. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң old Anchor generated codeлҠ” composite/nested account structм—җ мқҙ overrideлҘј лҒқк№Ңм§Җ м „нҢҢн•ҳм§Җ лӘ»н–ҲлӢӨ. кІ°кіјм ҒмңјлЎң нҳём¶ңмһҗлҠ” "signerлҘј лӮҙл ёлӢӨ"кі  мғқк°Ғн•ҳм§Җл§Ң, мӨ‘мІ© кі„м •м—җм„ңлҠ” signer bitк°Җ к·ёлҢҖлЎң мӮҙм•„лӮЁм•„ мҷёл¶Җ CPIлЎң мғҲм–ҙ лӮҳк°Ҳ мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. victim proxy/router/off-chain builder constructs forwarded metas for an external instruction and explicitly requests signer downgrade via `to_account_metas(Some(false))` (or equivalent wrapper expectation).
2. affected Anchor generated code recurses into composite accounts with `None` instead of the requested override, and nested signer-bearing fields fall back to default signer semantics.
3. forwarded instruction reaches an external program, plugin, adapter, or later CPI hop with signer privilege unexpectedly preserved on an authority/user/PDA-adjacent account.
4. external program takes a branch that should have been impossible after signer stripping: initialize, approve, mutate, withdraw, delegate, or reconfigure.
5. team misdiagnoses the incident because the call site visibly asked to disable signer propagation, so reviewers trust the abstraction instead of inspecting the serialized `AccountMeta` vector.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A70** = callerк°Җ signer privilegeлҘј мӢӨмҲҳлЎң нҳ№мқҖ л¬ҙмӢ¬мҪ” мҷёл¶Җ CPIм—җ м§Ғм ‘ м „лӢ¬н•ҳлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ.
- **A117** = callerк°Җ **л¶„лӘ…нһҲ signerлҘј м ңкұ°н•ҳл Өкі  мӢңлҸ„н–ҲлҠ”лҚ°**, framework-generated serialization кІҪкі„м—җм„ң к·ё downgradeк°Җ мӨ‘мІ© кі„м •м—җ л°ҳмҳҒлҗҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ. мҰү "к¶Ңн•ң м „лӢ¬" мқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **к¶Ңн•ң м ңкұ° мӢӨнҢЁ** к°Җ н•өмӢ¬мқҙлӢӨ.
- **A113** = Token-2022 CPI Guard owner pubkey confusion к°ҷмқҖ owner-field wiring л¬ём ң.
- **A117** = owner к°’мқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **serialized `AccountMeta.is_signer` provenance** л¬ём ңлӢӨ.
- **A112** = IDL / tooling metadata trust-boundary confusion.
- **A117** = generated client/account-meta emission pathмқҳ **runtime privilege-shaping bug** лӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. к°җмӮ¬мһҗлҠ” ліҙнҶө on-chain instruction logicлҘј ліҙм§Җ, generated `to_account_metas` code pathк№Ңм§Җ лӮҙл Өк°Җм§ҖлҠ” м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
2. нҸүнҸүн•ң(flat) кі„м • кө¬мЎ°м—җм„ңлҠ” overrideк°Җ м •мғҒ лҸҷмһ‘н•  мҲҳ мһҲм–ҙ, **composite/nested accounts** м—җм„ңл§Ң л“ңлҹ¬лӮҳлҠ” нҡҢк·ҖлҘј лҶ“м№ҳкё° мүҪлӢӨ.
3. лҰ¬л·°м–ҙлҠ” `Some(false)` к°ҷмқҖ нҳём¶ң нқ”м Ғмқ„ ліҙл©ҙ мқҙлҜё signer м ңкұ°к°Җ мҷ„лЈҢлҗҗлӢӨкі  мӢ¬лҰ¬м ҒмңјлЎң кІ°лЎ лӮҙлҰ¬кё° мүҪлӢӨ.
4. н…ҢмҠӨнҠёк°Җ honest target + direct accounts мЎ°н•©м—җ м№ҳмҡ°м№ҳл©ҙ, proxy/remaining-accounts forwarding кІҪкі„мқҳ privilege leakлҘј мһ¬нҳ„н•ҳм§Җ лӘ»н•ңлӢӨ.

**PoC scenario (future Solana adapter pattern)**:
```rust
// team believes signer privilege is stripped before forwarding
let mut metas = external::client::accounts::Init {
    authority,
    vault,
    system_program,
}.to_account_metas(Some(false));

// old generated path may preserve signer on nested/composite fields
invoke(&Instruction { program_id: external_program, accounts: metas, data }, infos)?;

// external program unexpectedly still sees authority as signer
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- AnchorлҘј `#3322` fix нҸ¬н•Ё лІ„м „мңјлЎң мҳ¬лҰҙ кІғ
- external CPI / proxy / adapter кІҪлЎңм—җм„ңлҠ” "нҳём¶ңл¶Җм—җм„ң `Some(false)` лҘј мӨ¬лӢӨ" лҘј мҰқкұ°лЎң мӮјм§Җ л§җкі , **мөңмў… serialized `AccountMeta` list** м—җ unexpected signerк°Җ м—ҶлҠ”м§Җ лӢЁмң„ н…ҢмҠӨнҠёлЎң кІҖмҰқн•  кІғ
- composite account struct / nested accounts / remaining-accounts forwardingмқҙ мһҲлҠ” кІҪлЎңлҠ” signer downgrade regression testлҘј лі„лҸ„лЎң л‘ҳ кІғ
- privilege downgradingмқҙ мӨ‘мҡ”н•ң кІҪлЎңм—җм„ңлҠ” generated helperлҘј л§№мӢ н•ҳм§Җ л§җкі , н•„мҡ”н•ң кІҪмҡ° `AccountMeta` лҘј мҲҳлҸҷмңјлЎң кө¬м„ұн•  кІғ
- мҷёл¶Җ н”„лЎңк·ёлһЁм—җ л„ҳкё°лҠ” authority/user/PDA кі„м •мқҖ signer мң м§Җк°Җ мқҳлҸ„лҗң кІҪмҡ°л§Ң allowlist-styleлЎң н—Ҳмҡ©н•  кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/Cargo.toml` лҠ” `anchor-lang = "0.31.1"`, `anchor-spl = "0.31.1"` лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳкі , `keeper/Cargo.toml` лҠ” `anchor-client = "0.31.1"` лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ңлӢӨ.
- к·ёлҹ¬лӮҳ нҳ„мһ¬ мҠӨмә”н•ң `programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` / `keeper/src/` кІҪлЎңм—җм„ңлҠ” `declare_program!`, `to_account_metas`, generic proxy forwarding, nested account-meta builder usageлҘј м°ҫм§Җ лӘ»н–ҲлӢӨ.
- мҰү **dependency signalмқҖ мЎҙмһ¬н•ҳм§Җл§Ң current exploit pathлҠ” лҜёнҷ•мқё** мқҙлӢӨ.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ Microstableмқҙ router/adapter/plugin/proxy instructionмқҙлӮҳ generated-client based forwardingмқ„ лҸ„мһ…н•ҳл©ҙ A117мқҖ мҰүмӢң нҷңм„ұнҷ”лҗ  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A117 Anchor Composite AccountMeta Signer-Override Drop / Privilege Downgrade Bypass | explicit signer-downgrade request (`to_account_metas(Some(false))`) fails to propagate through composite/nested account serialization, preserving unexpected signer bits on forwarded metas | proxy/adapter privilege leak, impossible-after-downgrade branches become reachable, external CPI authority misuse despite apparent signer stripping | repo is on Anchor `0.31.1`, but current code scan found no direct meta-forwarding path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, future router/adapter/proxy features must regression-test serialized signer bits |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-20 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A117** added after reclassifying Anchor `#3322` from an adjacent signal into an independent privilege-downgrade-bypass vector. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless meta-forwarding/proxy flows are introduced.

## 2026-04-22 Anchor Account-Parser Collision Pattern Addition

### D52. Anchor Composite Account-Group Name Collision / Instruction Parser Ambiguity Smuggle

**Source signals (2026-04-22 sweep)**:
- `solana-foundation/anchor` commit `df44381` / PR `#4401` (`Fix name collision in composite account de-duplicator`, 2026-04-21)
- upstream description: composite de-duplicator compared newly collected composite groups only against prior composites, not against top-level instruction account entries, allowing duplicate generated names or duplicate account-group definitions in final parser input

**Key insight**: нҢҖмқҖ ліҙнҶө "IDL/кі„м • л©”нғҖлҠ” н”„л Ҳмһ„мӣҢнҒ¬к°Җ мғқм„ұн–ҲмңјлӢҲ лӮҙл¶Җ мқјкҙҖм„ұк№Ңм§Җ ліҙмһҘлҗңлӢӨ" кі  к°Җм •н•ңлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң мқҙ лІ„к·ёлҠ” **мң„мЎ°лҗң metadataлҘј мЈјмһ…н•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•„лҸ„**, honest-generated output лӮҙл¶Җм—җм„ң composite account groupкіј vanilla instruction accountк°Җ к°ҷмқҖ мқҙлҰ„мқҙлӮҳ к°ҷмқҖ layoutлЎң м¶©лҸҢн•ҙ **instruction parser / generated clientк°Җ м• л§Өн•ң м •мқҳлҘј мҶҢл№„н•ҳлҸ„лЎқ** л§Ңл“Ө мҲҳ мһҲмқҢмқ„ ліҙм—¬мӨҖлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. attacker-controlled repo change, compromised contributor, or unsafe upstream dependency introduces a composite account shape whose generated name/layout collides with a normal instruction account entry that appears later in the emitted output.
2. vulnerable de-duplicator keeps both definitions because it only checked composite-vs-composite collisions, not composite-vs-top-level instruction entries.
3. downstream parser, generated client, validator helper, or policy engine reuses the final output and resolves the duplicate name/group ambiguously (first-wins, last-wins, merge, or silent overwrite).
4. operator or integration code believes it is validating/signing one account schema, while the effective parsed structure maps to another group with different account ordering, mutability, or semantic meaning.
5. runtime program logic may stay unchanged, yet off-chain admission, signing, simulation, or policy enforcement is steered onto the wrong trust boundary.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D31 / A112** = metadata source authenticity or trust-boundary confusionмқҙлӢӨ.
- **D52** = sourceлҠ” honest-generated artifactмқј мҲҳ мһҲм§Җл§Ң, **artifact лӮҙл¶Җмқҳ duplicate account-group identity** к°Җ parser ambiguityлҘј л§Ңл“ лӢӨ.
- **A117** = signer privilege downgrade serialization failureлӢӨ.
- **D52** = privilege bit мһҗмІҙк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј, **instruction/account-schema selection ambiguity** к°Җ н•өмӢ¬мқҙлӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. к°җмӮ¬мһҗлҠ” лҢҖк°ң on-chain Rust logicмҷҖ IDL мЎҙмһ¬ м—¬л¶ҖлҠ” ліҙм§Җл§Ң, generated account-group namespace collisionк№Ңм§ҖлҠ” мһҳ ліҙм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
2. duplicate nameмқҙ мғқкІЁлҸ„ buildк°Җ мҰүмӢң к№Ём§Җм§Җ м•Ҡмңјл©ҙ, parser consumerлі„ first-wins/last-wins м°Ёмқҙк°Җ л’ӨлҠҰкІҢ л¬ём ңлҘј л“ңлҹ¬лӮёлӢӨ.
3. нҢҖмқҖ "н”„л Ҳмһ„мӣҢнҒ¬к°Җ л§Ңл“  м¶ңл Ҙ" мқҙлқјлҠ” мқҙмң лЎң мӮ°м¶ңл¬ј лӮҙл¶Җ мқјкҙҖм„ұ кІҖмҰқмқ„ мғқлһөн•ҳкё° мүҪлӢӨ.
4. exploitмқҖ лҹ°нғҖмһ„ мҪ”л“ң ліҖмЎ°к°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј **client/parser/policy plane мҳӨлҸ„** лЎң лӮҳнғҖлӮҳм„ң, мӮ¬кі  нӣ„м—җлҸ„ root causeлҘј business logicм—җм„ңл§Ң м°ҫкё° мүҪлӢӨ.

**PoC scenario (future Anchor parser/client migration)**:
```rust
// generated parser output contains two account groups with effectively colliding identity
// downstream tooling resolves one implicitly
let parsed = instruction_parser.parse(ix_data, emitted_accounts)?;

// operator believes `parsed.accounts.authority` came from the canonical top-level schema
// but parser actually bound the colliding composite definition
sign_or_validate(parsed)?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- Anchor `#4401` fix нҸ¬н•Ё лІ„м „мңјлЎң мҳ¬лҰҙ кІғ
- generated IDL / parser input / account-group metadataм—җм„ң **duplicate group name, duplicate layout alias, ambiguous instruction-account mapping** мқ„ CIм—җм„ң мӢӨнҢЁ мІҳлҰ¬н•  кІғ
- parser consumerл§ҲлӢӨ first-wins/last-wins/overwrite semanticsлҘј н…ҢмҠӨнҠёлЎң кі м •н•  кІғ
- "generated by framework" лҘј л¬ҙмҳӨлҘҳ к°Җм •мңјлЎң мӮјм§Җ л§җкі , emitted account-group namespace diffлҘј лҰҙлҰ¬мҠӨ м•„нӢ°нҢ©нҠё кІҖмҰқ н•ӯлӘ©м—җ л„Јмқ„ кІғ
- migration мӢң old/new parser кІ°кіјлҘј к°ҷмқҖ instruction corpusм—җ лҢҖн•ҙ diff н•ҳм—¬ account ordering, mutability, signer expectationмқҙ л°”лҖҢм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлҠ”м§Җ нҷ•мқён•  кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/package.json` мқҖ `@coral-xyz/anchor = ^0.31.1` мқҙкі , н…ҢмҠӨнҠёлҠ” `target/types/microstable` + `anchor.workspace.microstable` лҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ңлӢӨ.
- нҳ„мһ¬ л ҲнҸ¬м—җм„ңлҠ” Anchor 1.0 кі„м—ҙмқҳ new instruction parser / composite account-group parser migration нқ”м Ғмқ„ м°ҫм§Җ лӘ»н–ҲлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ Anchor parser/client generation migrationмқҙлӮҳ richer composite account schema лҸ„мһ… мӢң, generated artifact diffмҷҖ duplicate-group lintк°Җ н•„мҲҳлӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D52 Anchor Composite Account-Group Name Collision / Instruction Parser Ambiguity Smuggle | composite-account de-duplicator fails to compare composite groups against top-level instruction account entries, leaving duplicate generated names or duplicate group definitions in parser input | ambiguous client/parser binding, wrong account-schema validation, signing/policy checks applied to unintended account ordering or semantics | repo is still on Anchor `0.31.1` and current code scan found no Anchor 1.0 parser migration path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but future parser/client upgrades must lint duplicate group identities and diff generated schemas |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-22 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D52** added from Anchor `#4401` after classifying it as a parser-plane ambiguity vector rather than a mere metadata hygiene bug. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless parser/client migration is introduced.

## 2026-04-24 zkVM Guest-Parser Forgery Pattern Addition

### A118. zkVM Guest Unchecked Deserialization / Enum Jump-Table Proof Forgery

**Source signals (2026-04-24 sweep)**:
- Trail of Bits, `We beat GoogleвҖҷs zero-knowledge proof of quantum cryptanalysis` (2026-04-17)
- Google patch reference in updated paper / disclosure trail

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ zk ліҙм•Ҳ м җкІҖмқ„ н•  л•Ң verifying key, public input binding, ceremony transcript к°ҷмқҖ **м•”нҳён•ҷм Ғ verifier кІҪкі„** л§Ң ліёлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң мқҙ мӮ¬лЎҖлҠ” verifierмҷҖ verification keyк°Җ к·ёлҢҖлЎңм—¬лҸ„, **proofлҘј л§Ңл“ңлҠ” guest program / simulator / parser мӘҪм—җм„ң unsafe deserializationкіј enum dispatch confusionмқҙ мһҲмңјл©ҙ лҸҷмқј verification key м•„лһҳм„ңлҸ„ forged proofк°Җ л§Ңл“Өм–ҙм§Ҳ мҲҳ мһҲмқҢ** мқ„ ліҙм—¬мӨҖлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol or research system accepts attacker-controlled private bytes that are meant to deserialize into guest-program instructions / operation vectors.
2. guest code uses unchecked deserialization (`access_unchecked`, unsafe casts, unchecked archive access, or equivalent) because proof cost or runtime cost makes validation look expensive.
3. malformed bytes inject an out-of-range enum / opcode / variant tag that survives into the simulator or prover execution path.
4. compiled jump table / dispatch logic takes an unintended branch, so accounting, bounds, or semantic checks are skipped while the execution result still looks acceptable enough to satisfy the final statement.
5. prover emits a proof that verifies under the **same verification key** as the honest proof, so downstream verifiers cannot distinguish forged execution from a legitimate optimization.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A49 / A87** = trusted setup or verifier-constant collapseлӢӨ.
- **A50** = Fiat-Shamir transcriptм—җм„ң public claim bindingмқҙ л№ м§„ нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ.
- **A118** = verifier mathлӮҳ VK driftк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј, **guest/prover implementation itself** к°Җ malformed private bytesлҘј мһҳлӘ» л°ӣм•„л“Өм—¬ proofлҘј мң„мЎ°н•ҳлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ.
- мҰү A118мқҳ н•өмӢ¬мқҖ **same-VK proof forgery via unsafe guest parsing / dispatch confusion** мқҙлӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. л§ҺмқҖ к°җмӮ¬ лІ”мң„к°Җ verifier contract / proof system spec / setup ceremonyм—җ м№ҳмҡ°міҗ мһҲкі , proving guestлӮҳ simulator мҪ”л“ңлҠ” м„ұлҠҘ мҪ”л“ңлЎң м·Ёкёүлҗҳм–ҙ м–•кІҢ ліёлӢӨ.
2. unsafe deserializationмқҖ "proof generation cost м Ҳк°җ" мқҙлқјлҠ” лӘ…л¶„мңјлЎң н•©лҰ¬нҷ”лҗҳкё° мүҪлӢӨ.
3. enum discriminant / jump-table UBлҠ” м–ём–ҙ мҲҳмӨҖ undefined behaviorмҷҖ proving semanticsк°Җ кІ№міҗ мһ¬нҳ„мқҙ м–ҙл өлӢӨ.
4. мӮ¬кі  нӣ„м—җлҸ„ нҢҖмқҖ "verification keyлҸ„ к°ҷкі  verifierлҸ„ м•Ҳ л°”лҖҢм—ҲлҠ”лҚ° мҷң мң„мЎ°к°Җ лҗҳм§Җ" лқјлҠ” мӢ¬лҰ¬м Ғ н•Ём •м—җ л№ м§Җкё° мүҪлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE: attacker-controlled private bytes trusted as a valid archived structure
let private_bytes = zkvm::io::read_vec();
let ops = unsafe {
    rkyv::access_unchecked::<rkyv::Archived<Vec<Op>>>(&private_bytes)
};

for op in ops.iter() {
    match op.kind {
        OpKind::CriticalGate => stats.critical += 1,
        OpKind::CheapGate => stats.cheap += 1,
        _ => {}
    }

    // second dispatch can jump to unintended branch when enum tag is invalid
    execute(op);
}

// SAFER: validate untrusted bytes before guest execution, reject invalid enum tags,
// and keep counting / execution over the same checked representation
let private_bytes = zkvm::io::read_vec();
let ops = rkyv::from_bytes::<Vec<Op>>(&private_bytes)?;
for op in &ops {
    op.validate()?;
    execute_checked(op, &mut stats)?;
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- proving guest / simulatorлҸ„ verifierмҷҖ к°ҷмқҖ мҲҳмӨҖмқҳ ліҙм•Ҳ к°җмӮ¬ лІ”мң„м—җ нҸ¬н•Ён•  кІғ
- private witness bytesм—җ лҢҖн•ҙ unchecked deserializationмқ„ кёҲм§Җн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ, мөңмҶҢн•ң enum tag / length / pointer-offset validationмқ„ лі„лҸ„ м„ н–үн•  кІғ
- cost accountingкіј semantic executionмқ„ м„ңлЎң лӢӨлҘё unchecked dispatchм—җ лӮҳлҲ„м§Җ л§җкі , **validated IR** н•ң лІҢлЎң кіөмң н•  кІғ
- лҸҷмқј verification key м•„лһҳм„ң forged proofк°Җ к°ҖлҠҘн•ңм§Җ ліҙлҠ” negative testлҘј л‘ҳ кІғ (false low-cost / false bounded-resource / false state-transition proof)
- `unsafe` лҘј proof-cost м Ҳк°җ мӮ¬мң лЎң мӮ¬мҡ©н•  кІҪмҡ°, UBк°Җ proof statementлЎң м „мқҙлҗҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”м§Җ лі„лҸ„ мҰқлӘ… лҳҗлҠ” adversarial fuzzлҘј мҡ”кө¬н•  кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- нҳ„мһ¬ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мҷҖ `keeper/src/` м—җлҠ” SP1/RISC0/Groth16 guest verifierлӮҳ zkVM proving pathк°Җ м—ҶлӢӨ.
- `keeper/src/hermes.rs` мқҳ `Proof::WormholeMerkle` лҠ” accumulator / merkle proof мІҳлҰ¬лЎң, мқҙлІҲ A118мқҳ zkVM guest-forgery нҢЁн„ҙкіјлҠ” лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ proof-backed reserve attestations, zk oracle attestations, zk coprocessor, privacy-preserving solvency proofsлҘј л¶ҷмқҙл©ҙ A118мқҖ мҰүмӢң relevant н•ҙм§„лӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A118 zkVM Guest Unchecked Deserialization / Enum Jump-Table Proof Forgery | attacker-controlled private bytes are deserialized without validation inside a zkVM guest/prover; invalid enum/opcode tags trigger unintended dispatch so accounting or semantic checks are skipped, yet the emitted proof still verifies under the same verification key | same-VK forged proof, false resource bounds, false execution attestations, proof-backed mint/oracle/reserve fraud without VK drift | current Microstable code has no zkVM proving path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future zk-backed attestation / proof coprocessor design must audit guest parser safety as a first-class trust boundary |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-24 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A118** added after classifying the Trail of Bits same-VK forged-proof result as a guest/prover implementation vector, not a verifier-key or transcript-binding bug. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless zk-backed proving components are introduced.

## 2026-04-28 Immutable Legacy Package Pattern Addition

### A119. Immutable Legacy Package / Shared-State Version-Gate Bypass

**Source signals (2026-04-28 sweep)**:
- Scallop / sSUI rewards incident coverage (incident 2026-04-26, public mechanism write-ups 2026-04-27)

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ мғҲ UI, мғҲ SDK, мғҲ package/versionмңјлЎң мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗлҘј мҳ®кё°л©ҙ мҳҲм „ кІҪлЎңлҠ” мӮ¬мӢӨмғҒ мЈҪм—ҲлӢӨкі  лҠҗлӮҖлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң **мҳҲм „ л°°нҸ¬ліёмқҙ мІҙмқё мң„м—җ к·ёлҢҖлЎң лӮЁкі , к°ҷмқҖ shared object / shared state / rewards pool / vault authorityлҘј кі„мҶҚ л§Ңм§Ҳ мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨл©ҙ, deprecated pathлҠ” м—¬м „нһҲ live privileged code** лӢӨ. мқҙлІҲ Scallop мӮ¬лЎҖмқҳ н•өмӢ¬мқҖ лӢЁмҲңн•ң `last_index` мҙҲкё°нҷ” лІ„к·ёл§Ңмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј, **к·ё лІ„к·ёк°Җ лӮЁм•„ мһҲлҠ” мҳӣ packageк°Җ м—¬м „нһҲ нҳ„мһ¬ rewards stateм—җ м“°кё° к¶Ңн•ңмқ„ к°Җм§„ мұ„ нҳём¶ң к°ҖлҠҘн–ҲлӢӨ** лҠ” м җмқҙлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol migrates users to a newer package / contract / module and assumes routing through the new path is sufficient retirement.
2. old package remains immutable and callable on-chain.
3. shared objects / shared pools / shared PDAs / shared mint authority are not version-gated to the current package only.
4. attacker calls the deprecated path directly, bypassing current SDK/UI restrictions.
5. latent bug in the old path (uninitialized accumulator/index, weaker auth, stale invariant, outdated math) mutates live shared state or extracts current rewards/assets.

**2026-05 reinforcement (Renegade + Huma + Transit + Aurellion)**:
- **Renegade V1 (2026-05-10)**: legacy Arbitrum deploymentк°Җ faulty migrationмңјлЎң version counterк°Җ м–ҙкёӢлӮң мұ„ лӮЁм•„ мһҲм—Ҳкі , unprotected initializerк°Җ лӢӨмӢң м—ҙлҰ¬л©ҙм„ң deprecated V1 мһҗмІҙк°Җ live drain pathк°Җ лҗҗлӢӨ.
- **Huma Finance V1 (2026-05-11)**: нҢҖмқҖ мқҙлҜё V2лЎң л„ҳм–ҙк°Җкі  V1мқ„ sunset мӨ‘мқҙм—Ҳм§Җл§Ң, deprecated BaseCreditPoolм—җ лӮЁмқҖ protocol/pool-owner fee balanceлҠ” кі„мҶҚ value-bearing surfaceмҳҖлӢӨ.
- **Transit Finance legacy TRON contract (2026-05-13)**: нҳ„мһ¬ кі„м•Ҫмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **2022-era deprecated contract** к°Җ лі„лҸ„лЎң мӮҙм•„ мһҲм—Ҳкі , к·ё мһ”м—¬ кІҪлЎңл§ҢмңјлЎңлҸ„ $1.88Mмқҙ л№ м ёлӮҳк°”лӢӨ.
- **Aurellion Labs Diamond Proxy (2026-05-12)**: re-initialization мһҗмІҙлҠ” нҳ„мһ¬ к¶Ңн•ң нғҲм·Ё лІЎн„°мҳҖм§Җл§Ң, мӢӨм ң мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗ мҶҗмӢӨмқҖ **мҳҲм „м—җ лӮЁм•„ мһҲлҚҳ ERC-20 approvals** к°Җ кі„мҶҚ live authorityмҳҖкё° л•Ңл¬ём—җ л°ңмғқн–ҲлӢӨ.

кіөнҶөм җмқҖ `old surface is no longer the intended path` мҷҖ `old surface can no longer move value` к°Җ лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨлҠ” м җмқҙлӢӨ. мҰү A119лҠ” immutable packageл§Ңмқҳ нҠ№мҲҳ мӮ¬лЎҖк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј, **legacy execution path + residual authority persistence** мқјл°ҳ кө¬мЎ°мқҳ лҢҖн‘ң кё°мҲ  лІЎн„°лӢӨ.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A10 Logic Bug** лҠ” нҠ№м • н•ЁмҲҳ лӮҙл¶Җ кІ°н•Ё мһҗмІҙлҘј л§җн•ңлӢӨ. **A119** лҠ” к·ё кІ°н•Ёмқҙ **retired лҗҳм—Ҳм–ҙм•ј н•  legacy execution pathк°Җ м•„м§Ғ live authorityлҘј мң м§Җ** н•ҳкё° л•Ңл¬ём—җ мӢӨм ң мҶҗмӢӨлЎң мқҙм–ҙм§ҖлҠ” кө¬мЎ°лҘј лӢӨлЈ¬лӢӨ.
- **B69 Upgrade Asynchrony** лҠ” н”„лЎңнҶ мҪң AмҷҖ мҷёл¶Җ нҢҢнҠёл„Ҳ/нҶөн•© кІҪкі„мқҳ л№„лҸҷкё° м—…к·ёл Ҳмқҙл“ң мұ…мһ„ кіөл°ұмқҙлӢӨ. **A119** лҠ” **к°ҷмқҖ н”„лЎңнҶ мҪң лӮҙл¶Җмқҳ кө¬лІ„м „ package / sidecar / module** мқҙ shared stateлҘј кі„мҶҚ кұҙл“ңлҰҙ мҲҳ мһҲлҠ” кө¬мЎ°лӢӨ.
- **A119мқҳ н•өмӢ¬мқҖ stale code мһҗмІҙліҙлӢӨ stale codeмқҳ к¶Ңн•ң м§ҖмҶҚм„ұ** мқҙлӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. к°җмӮ¬ лІ”мң„к°Җ current package / current entrypoint / current SDKм—җ м§‘мӨ‘лҗҳкі , кіјкұ° л°°нҸ¬ліёмқҖ "unused" лқјлҠ” мҡҙмҳҒ к°Җм •мңјлЎң л°Җл ӨлӮңлӢӨ.
2. immutable-chain нҷҳкІҪм—җм„ңлҠ” old codeлҘј м§Җмҡ°м§Җ лӘ»н•ҳм§Җл§Ң, many teams mentally treat UI migration as decommission.
3. shared object or shared-state к¶Ңн•ңмқҙ version field м—Ҷмқҙ лӮЁм•„ мһҲм–ҙлҸ„ happy-path testingмқҖ мөңмӢ  кІҪлЎңл§Ң нғҖлҜҖлЎң лҶ“м№ҳкё° мүҪлӢӨ.
4. мӮ¬кі  нӣ„м—җлҸ„ нҢҖмқҖ "core contracts are safe" лқјкі  л§җн•ҳкё° мү¬мӣҢ, мӢӨм ң privileged legacy surfaceк°Җ лҠҰкІҢ мһ¬л¶„лҘҳлҗңлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```move
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: deprecated package can still mutate live shared state.
public entry fun claim_legacy_rewards(pool: &mut RewardsPool, user: &mut UserSpool) {
    // MISSING: assert!(pool.active_package == CURRENT_PACKAGE_ID, E_DEPRECATED_PATH);
    // MISSING: assert!(user.last_index_initialized, E_UNINITIALIZED_INDEX);

    let delta = pool.spool_index - user.last_index;
    let reward = delta * user.staked_amount;
    transfer_rewards(pool, reward);
}

// SAFER SHAPE: bind shared state to the active version/package and reject retired paths.
public entry fun claim_rewards(pool: &mut RewardsPool, user: &mut UserSpool) {
    assert!(pool.active_package == CURRENT_PACKAGE_ID, E_DEPRECATED_PATH);
    assert!(user.last_index_initialized, E_UNINITIALIZED_INDEX);
    ...
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- migration нӣ„м—җлҸ„ old package / old program / old sidecarк°Җ live shared stateм—җ м ‘к·ј к°ҖлҠҘн•ңм§Җ лі„лҸ„ inventoryлҘј л‘ҳ кІғ
- shared object / PDA / vault authorityм—җ **active version / active program binding** мқ„ л‘җкі , retired pathлҠ” hard rejectн•  кІғ
- "SDK no longer routes there" лҘј retirement мҰқкұ°лЎң м·Ёкёүн•ҳм§Җ л§җ кІғ
- мқҙм „ л°°нҸ¬ліём—җ latent bugк°Җ мһҲм–ҙлҸ„ current stateлҘј лӘ» л§Ңм§ҖкІҢ **authority migration / state migration / access revocation** мқ„ н•Ёк»ҳ мҷ„лЈҢн•  кІғ
- audit checklistм—җ **historical live entrypoint reachability** лҘј лі„лҸ„ н•ӯлӘ©мңјлЎң м¶”к°Җн•  кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- нҳ„мһ¬ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` лҠ” лӢЁмқј `declare_id!` кё°л°ҳ м—…к·ёл Ҳмқҙл“ңнҳ• Solana program кІҪлЎңлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ңлӢӨ.
- repo scan кё°мӨҖ, нҳ„мһ¬ мҪ”л“ңлІ мқҙмҠӨм—җлҠ” **parallel retired program ID**, legacy rewards sidecar, or old module that still writes the same live shared state under a different on-chain package pathк°Җ нҷ•мқёлҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ rewards / bridge / attestation / auxiliary programмқ„ **мғҲ program IDлЎң л¶„лҰ¬ л§Ҳмқҙк·ёл Ҳмқҙм…ҳ** н•ҳл©ҙм„ң shared PDA/vault authorityлҘј мһ¬мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©ҙ, A119лҠ” мҰүмӢң мһ¬нҸүк°Җ лҢҖмғҒмқҙ лҗңлӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A119 Immutable Legacy Package / Shared-State Version-Gate Bypass | deprecated immutable package/program remains callable and still has authority over current shared state, so an old latent bug can drain live rewards/assets despite current SDK/UI using a newer path | legacy-path drain, reward inflation, stale-auth bypass, post-migration false sense of safety | current Microstable scan shows a single upgradeable Solana program path and no parallel retired write-capable package; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future multi-program migration must bind shared PDAs/vaults to the active version |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-28 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A119** added after Scallop made the mechanism public enough to separate **legacy-package reachability + shared-state authority persistence** from a generic logic bug. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A119 itself**; the open **B45 audit-attestation gap** remains the top current HIGH continuity issue.

## 2026-04-29 Multi-Hop Route Minimum Aggregation Pattern Addition

### A120. Multi-Hop Route Minimum Aggregation / Terminal-Settlement Mismatch

**Source signals (2026-04-29 sweep)**:
- Rhea Finance / Burrowland postmortem coverage (incident 2026-04-16, fuller public mechanism synthesized by 2026-04-28)
- Burrowland source links referenced in the postmortem:
  - `margin_trading.rs#get_token_out()` `#L101-L113`
  - `margin_position.rs#is_min_amount_out_reasonable()` `#L268-L456`
  - `margin_trading.rs#on_open_trade_return()` `#L328-L398`

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ н”„лЎңнҶ мҪңмқҙ multi-hop swap routeмқҳ `min_amount_out` лҘј "мөңмў… мӮ°м¶ң ліҙмһҘк°’"мІҳлҹј лӢӨлЈ¬лӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң route parserк°Җ **мӨ‘к°„ hopмқҳ minimumк№Ңм§Җ лӘЁл‘җ мөңмў… outputмІҳлҹј н•©мӮ°** н•ҳкұ°лӮҳ, execution callbackм—җм„ң **мӢӨм ң terminal outputмқҙ к·ё minimumмқ„ л§ҢмЎұн–ҲлҠ”м§Җ лӢӨмӢң кІҖмҰқн•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡмңјл©ҙ**, slippage/health checksлҠ” мЎҙмһ¬н•ҙлҸ„ м „л¶Җ к°Җм§ң м•Ҳм „мһҘм№ҳк°Җ лҗңлӢӨ. мқҙлІҲ Rhea мӮ¬лЎҖмқҳ н•өмӢ¬мқҖ к°Җм§ң мһҗмӮ°л§Ңмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј, **route semanticsлҘј мһҳлӘ» мқҪмқҖ admission logic + settlement-time recheck л¶Җмһ¬** мҳҖлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. attacker creates or controls a multi-hop route whose intermediate asset appears repeatedly.
2. route parser derives the "minimum expected output" by summing `min_amount_out` values from each hop instead of isolating the true terminal output.
3. protocol compares that inflated figure against endpoint-asset oracle checks and declares the route reasonable.
4. DEX executes faithfully and returns only a tiny real terminal amount.
5. settlement callback credits whatever arrived, but does **not** recheck `actual_terminal_output >= validated_minimum` and does not recompute health/solvency before finalizing success.
6. attacker opens an undercollateralized position or receives real assets against effectively worthless output, then liquidation / reserve depletion magnifies losses.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A98** лҠ” fake asset / attacker-created marketк°Җ oracle or admission layerлҘј мҶҚмқҙлҠ” л¬ём ңлӢӨ. **A120** мқҖ мһҗмӮ°мқҙ л¬ҙм—Үмқҙл“  мғҒкҙҖм—Ҷмқҙ, **multi-hop route semanticsмҷҖ final settlement accountingмқҙ м–ҙкёӢлӮҳлҠ” кІғ мһҗмІҙ** к°Җ н•өмӢ¬мқҙлӢӨ.
- **B35 / A63** лҠ” slippage parameterк°Җ 0мқҙкұ°лӮҳ м§ҖлӮҳм№ҳкІҢ лҠҗмҠЁн•ң misconfigurationмқ„ лӢӨлЈ¬лӢӨ. **A120** мқҖ slippage checksк°Җ мЎҙмһ¬н•ҙлҸ„ **л¬ҙм—Үмқ„ minimumмңјлЎң кі„мӮ°н–ҲлҠ”м§Җ** к°Җ нӢҖлҰ¬л©ҙ к·ёлҢҖлЎң л¬ҙл Ҙнҷ”лҗңлӢӨлҠ” м җмқҙ лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ.
- **A10 Logic Bug** ліҙлӢӨ лҚ” мўҒкі  мһ¬нҳ„ к°ҖлҠҘн•ң нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ. н•өмӢ¬ primitiveлҠ” **intermediate-hop minimum aggregation + post-execution terminal-settlement mismatch** лӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. happy-path testsлҠ” ліҙнҶө single-hop лҳҗлҠ” м •мғҒ multi-hop routeл§Ң кІҖмҰқн•ҳкі , **к°ҷмқҖ intermediate assetмқҙ л°ҳліөлҗҳлҠ” route** лҘј adversarialн•ҳкІҢ л§Ңл“Өм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
2. admission checkмҷҖ execution callbackк°Җ м„ңлЎң лӢӨлҘё н•ЁмҲҳ/лӘЁл“Ҳм—җ лӮҳлүҳм–ҙ мһҲм–ҙ, auditorк°Җ "validated before swap" мҷҖ "settled after swap" мӮ¬мқҙмқҳ semantic continuityлҘј лҶ“м№ҳкё° мүҪлӢӨ.
3. oracle reasonableness checkк°Җ мһҲмңјл©ҙ нҢҖмқҙ к·ё мң„м—җ кіјмӢ н•ҳкё° мүҪм§Җл§Ң, **мһ…л Ҙлҗң minimum мһҗмІҙк°Җ inflated** лҗҳм–ҙ мһҲмңјл©ҙ oracle checkлҠ” м•Ҳм „мқ„ мҰқлӘ…н•ҳм§Җ лӘ»н•ңлӢӨ.
4. post-swap callbackлҠ” мў…мў… "success/failure bookkeeping" мңјлЎң м·Ёкёүлҗҳм–ҙ, мӢӨм ң output-vs-promised-output мһ¬кІҖмҰқмқҙ л№ м ёлҸ„ severityк°Җ лҠҰкІҢ л“ңлҹ¬лӮңлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: sums intermediate minima and never rechecks terminal output.
fn parse_route_minimum(route: &[SwapAction]) -> u128 {
    route.iter().map(|step| step.min_amount_out as u128).sum()
}

fn on_swap_return(actual_out: u128, validated_minimum: u128) {
    // MISSING: require!(actual_out >= validated_minimum);
    credit_position(actual_out);
    finalize_success();
}

// SAFER SHAPE: derive terminal-only minimum and recheck after execution.
fn parse_terminal_minimum(route: &[SwapAction]) -> u128 {
    route.last().map(|step| step.min_amount_out as u128).unwrap_or(0)
}

fn on_swap_return(actual_out: u128, validated_minimum: u128) {
    require!(actual_out >= validated_minimum, Error::SettlementShortfall);
    recompute_health_factor()?;
    finalize_success();
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- route parserлҠ” intermediate-hop minimaлҘј н•©мӮ°н•ҳм§Җ л§җкі  **terminal asset кё°мӨҖ** мңјлЎң normalize н•  кІғ
- route validation нӣ„ execution callbackм—җм„ң **actual output vs validated minimum** мқ„ л°ҳл“ңмӢң мһ¬кІҖмҰқн•  кІғ
- swap мҷ„лЈҢ нӣ„ position open / borrow finalize м „м—җ **health factor / collateral sufficiency** лҘј лӢӨмӢң кі„мӮ°н•  кІғ
- multi-hop route fuzzingм—җ repeated intermediate asset, fake pools, and late-settlement shortfall casesлҘј нҸ¬н•Ён•  кІғ
- attacker-created pools м°ЁлӢЁмқҖ м—¬м „нһҲ мӨ‘мҡ”н•ҳм§Җл§Ң, **trusted poolsл§ҢмңјлЎңлҸ„ A120мқҖ л°ңмғқн•  мҲҳ мһҲмқҢ** мқ„ лі„лҸ„ мқёмӢқн•  кІғ

**Sources**: https://rekt.news/rhea-finance-rekt | https://github.com/ref-finance/burrowland/blob/2110c7047f2d72a9189abcbaad5eafb4e4a87f9e/contracts/contract/src/margin_trading.rs#L101-L113 | https://github.com/ref-finance/burrowland/blob/2110c7047f2d72a9189abcbaad5eafb4e4a87f9e/contracts/contract/src/margin_position.rs#L268-L456 | https://github.com/ref-finance/burrowland/blob/2110c7047f2d72a9189abcbaad5eafb4e4a87f9e/contracts/contract/src/margin_trading.rs#L328-L398

**Microstable relevance**:
- нҳ„мһ¬ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мқҳ `rebalance()` лҠ” DEX route, multi-hop swap, callback settlementлҘј мҲҳн–үн•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡкі  **weight parameter update only** лҘј мІҳлҰ¬н•ңлӢӨ.
- `keeper/src/rebalance.rs` мҷҖ `keeper/src/wire.rs` лҸ„ `ix_rebalance(..., max_slippage_bps, ...)` лЎң on-chain weight update instructionл§Ң м „мҶЎн•ҳл©°, route path / `min_amount_out` / DEX aggregator calldataлҘј мӢЈм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ keeperк°Җ Jupiter/Orca/Raydium multi-hop swapмқ„ м§Ғм ‘ мЎ°лҰҪн•ҳкұ°лӮҳ margin-like delayed settlement flowлҘј л¶ҷмқҙл©ҙ A120мқҖ мҰүмӢң мһ¬нҸүк°Җ лҢҖмғҒмқҙлӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A120 Multi-Hop Route Minimum Aggregation / Terminal-Settlement Mismatch | protocol mis-parses multi-hop route minima by counting intermediate hops as terminal guarantees, then finalizes execution without rechecking actual terminal output against the validated minimum | undercollateralized position open, borrow against worthless output, reserve depletion, liquidation cascade despite nominal slippage/oracle checks | current Microstable code has no multi-hop route parser, no DEX callback settlement path, and `rebalance` only updates weights; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future swap-integrated keeper path must treat this as first-class |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-29 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A120** added after the fuller Rhea/Burrowland mechanism showed a reusable exploit class beyond generic fake-asset admission. Matrix is now **127+ named vectors + META-01~61 + B73~B78 = 188+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A120 itself**; **B45 audit-attestation gap** remains the top current HIGH continuity issue.

## 2026-05-01 Fixed-Polynomial Opening Omission Pattern Addition

### A121. Fixed-Polynomial Evaluation Opening Omission / Same-VK Proof Forgery

**Source signals (2026-05-01 KST sweep)**:
- OtterSec, `Unverified Evaluations in Dusk's PLONK` (published 2026-04-30)
- Dusk / Rusk public code links referenced in the write-up

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ zk verifierлҘј ліј л•Ң transcript binding, setup ceremony, public input hashingк№Ңм§Җл§Ң ліёлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң PLONKлҘҳ мӢңмҠӨн…ңм—җм„ңлҠ” verifierк°Җ мөңмў… л°©м •мӢқм—җ л„ЈлҠ” к°Ғ scalarк°Җ л°ҳл“ңмӢң (a) verifierк°Җ м§Ғм ‘ кі„мӮ°н–Ҳкұ°лӮҳ, (b) мқҙлҜё committed polynomialм—җ лҢҖн•ҙ **opening proofлЎң л¬¶мҳҖкұ°лӮҳ**, (c) verifier key commitmentлҘј м§Ғм ‘ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҙм•ј н•ңлӢӨ. Dusk мӮ¬лЎҖмқҳ н•өмӢ¬мқҖ proverк°Җ **fixed/public selector polynomialмқҳ evaluation к°’** мқ„ proof structм—җ мӢӨм–ҙ ліҙлғҲкі , verifierк°Җ к·ёкІғмқ„ final equationм—җ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©ҙм„ңлҸ„ **к·ё к°’мқҙ verifier-key commitmentмҷҖ мқјм№ҳн•ҳлҠ”м§Җ м „нҳҖ м—ҙм–ҙліҙм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ** лҠ” м җмқҙлӢӨ. к·ё мҲңк°„ н•ҙлӢ№ scalarлҠ” transcriptм—җ л¬¶мқё к°’мқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **attacker-controlled free variable** лЎң лҸҢм•„мҳЁлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol uses a PLONK-like verifier with fixed/public polynomials (selectors, lookup tables, verifier-key-backed polynomials) committed during preprocessing.
2. prover includes claimed evaluations of those fixed polynomials at challenge point `zeta` inside the proof payload.
3. verifier uses those scalars in linearization / quotient / final pairing equations.
4. however, verifier never checks an opening proof tying those evaluations back to the trusted commitments in the verifier key.
5. attacker chooses evaluation values that make the final equation pass, forging a proof for a false statement under the **same verification key**.
6. downstream application accepts a syntactically valid proof and releases value, admits invalid state transitions, or validates forged privacy actions.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A49** лҠ” trusted setup / verifying key constants мһҗмІҙк°Җ мһҳлӘ»лҗң кІҪмҡ°лӢӨ. **A121** мқҖ setup constantsк°Җ м •мғҒмқҙлҚ”лқјлҸ„, verifierк°Җ **proof м•Ҳмқҳ evaluation scalarлҘј commitment-bound valueлЎң кІҖмҰқн•ҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”** кө¬нҳ„ кІ°н•ЁмқҙлӢӨ.
- **A50** мқҖ Fiat-Shamir transcriptм—җ public claimмқҙ м ңл•Ң нқЎмҲҳлҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•„ statementк°Җ free variableмқҙ лҗҳлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ. **A121** мқҖ transcript orderingмқҙ л§һм•„лҸ„, **opening proofк°Җ л№ м§„ fixed-polynomial evaluation** мқҙ free variableмқҙ лҗңлӢӨлҠ” м җмқҙ лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ.
- **A118** мқҖ guest/prover parserмқҳ unsafe deserializationлЎң same-VK forgeryк°Җ мғқкё°лҠ” нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ. **A121** мқҖ proverк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј **verifier-side commitment-binding omission** мқҙлӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. л§ҺмқҖ к°җмӮ¬к°Җ witness polynomial openingмқҖ кјјкјјнһҲ ліҙм§Җл§Ң, selector / fixed polynomialмқҖ "м–ҙм°Ён”ј public circuit data" лқјкі  ліҙкі  prover-supplied evaluationмқ„ лҚң мқҳмӢ¬н•ңлӢӨ.
2. verifier codeк°Җ linearization / MSM / pairing checkлЎң кёём–ҙм§Җл©ҙ, м–ҙл–Ө scalarк°Җ locally derivedмқём§Җ, м–ҙл–Ө scalarк°Җ opening-boundмқём§Җ м¶”м Ғн•ҳкё°к°Җ кёүкІ©нһҲ м–ҙл ӨмӣҢм§„лӢӨ.
3. transcript bindingмқҙ л§һлҠ”м§Җл§Ң нҷ•мқён•ҳкі , **final equationм—җ л“Өм–ҙк°ҖлҠ” лӘЁл“  мҷёл¶Җ scalarк°Җ мӢӨм ң commitment-boundмқём§Җ** к№Ңм§ҖлҠ” мІҙнҒ¬лҰ¬мҠӨнҠём—җ м—ҶлҠ” кІҪмҡ°к°Җ л§ҺлӢӨ.
4. implementationмқҙ gnark к°ҷмқҖ м•Ҳм „н•ң кө¬мЎ°лҘј "л№„мҠ·н•ҳкІҢ" л”°лҘҙлҠ” кІғмІҳлҹј ліҙм—¬лҸ„, custom widget / batched opening мөңм Ғнҷ”к°Җ л“Өм–ҙк°Җл©ҙ мқҙ кІҪкі„к°Җ л¬ҙл„Ҳм§Җкё° мүҪлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE: verifier consumes prover-supplied fixed/selectors evaluations
// without opening them against verifier-key commitments.
let q_l_eval = proof.q_l_eval;
let q_r_eval = proof.q_r_eval;
let q_m_eval = proof.q_m_eval;
let q_o_eval = proof.q_o_eval;

let gate_term = q_m_eval * a_eval * b_eval
    + q_l_eval * a_eval
    + q_r_eval * b_eval
    + q_o_eval * c_eval;

verify_pairing(gate_term, witness_openings, quotient_opening)?; // MISSING: open(q_*, zeta)

// SAFER: every prover-supplied fixed/public evaluation must be commitment-bound.
verify_opening(vk.q_l_commitment, zeta, proof.q_l_eval, proof.q_l_opening)?;
verify_opening(vk.q_r_commitment, zeta, proof.q_r_eval, proof.q_r_opening)?;
verify_opening(vk.q_m_commitment, zeta, proof.q_m_eval, proof.q_m_opening)?;
verify_opening(vk.q_o_commitment, zeta, proof.q_o_eval, proof.q_o_opening)?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- final verifier equationм—җ л“Өм–ҙк°ҖлҠ” prover-supplied scalarлҘј м „л¶Җ л¶„лҘҳн•ҙ `local / opening-bound / commitment-direct` м…Ӣ мӨ‘ н•ҳлӮҳк°Җ м•„лӢҲл©ҙ мҰүмӢң кІҪкі н•  кІғ
- fixed/selectors/lookup-table evaluations лҸ„ witness evaluations мҷҖ лҸҷмқјн•ң мҲҳмӨҖмңјлЎң opening-proof кІҖмҰқмқ„ мҡ”кө¬н•  кІғ
- custom widget / batched opening / linearization optimizationмқ„ л„Јмқ„ л•Ң **"proofм—җм„ң мқҪм—Ҳм§Җл§Ң м—ҙм–ҙліҙм§Җ м•ҠмқҖ field"** diffлҘј CIм—җм„ң кІҖмӮ¬н•  кІғ
- same verification key м•„лһҳм„ң false statement proofлҘј мӢңлҸ„н•ҳлҠ” negative testлҘј л‘ҳ кІғ
- verifier лҰ¬л·°м—җм„ң "public polynomialмқҙлӢҲ м•Ҳм „" мқҙлқјлҠ” м§ҒкҙҖмқ„ кёҲм§Җн•ҳкі , мӢӨм ң binding pathлҘј н‘ңлЎң м¶”м Ғн•  кІғ

**Sources**: https://osec.io/blog/2026-04-30-unverified-evaluations-dusk-plonk/ | https://github.com/dusk-network/plonk | https://github.com/dusk-network/rusk

**Microstable relevance**:
- нҳ„мһ¬ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мҷҖ `keeper/src/` м—җлҠ” PLONK/Groth16/STARK verifier, proof-backed reserve attestation, zk coprocessor, or same-VK proof acceptance pathк°Җ м—ҶлӢӨ.
- `keeper/src/hermes.rs` мқҳ `Proof::WormholeMerkle` лҠ” Wormhole/Pyth accumulator-merkle verification кІҪкі„лЎң, мқҙлІҲ A121мқҳ **fixed-polynomial opening omission** кіјлҠ” лӢӨлҘё нҒҙлһҳмҠӨлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ zk-backed collateral attestations, proof-based solvency, privacy-preserving reserve statementsлҘј л¶ҷмқҙл©ҙ A121мқҖ мҰүмӢң relevant н•ҙм§„лӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A121 Fixed-Polynomial Evaluation Opening Omission / Same-VK Proof Forgery | verifier consumes prover-supplied evaluations of selector/fixed/public polynomials in the final proof equation without opening them against trusted commitments, turning those scalars into attacker-controlled free variables under the same verification key | forged proof for false statements, unbacked mint/withdraw, invalid shielded transfer admission, value release despite intact setup/transcript ordering | current Microstable code has no zk verifier or proof-acceptance path; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future zk-attestation integration must audit commitment-binding completeness as a first-class boundary |

**Matrix state as of 2026-05-01 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A121** added after the Dusk PLONK disclosure separated **verifier-side opening omission** from setup failures (A49), transcript-binding failures (A50), and guest-parser forgeries (A118). Matrix is now **128+ named vectors + META-01~63 + B73~B78 = 191+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A121 itself**; current result is future-facing only.

## 2026-05-14 Anchor Zero-Copy Validation Opt-Out Pattern Addition

### A122. Anchor Zero-Copy Validation Opt-Out / AccountLoader Trust Collapse

**Source signals (2026-05-14 KST sweep)**:
- Anchor commit `9d452e3` (`feat: allow bypassing owner/disc checks on zero copy accounts (#4162)`, merged 2026-05-13)
- Anchor PR `#4162` title history explicitly broadened from bypassing owner/discriminator checks to a supported API surface

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ Solana нҢҖмқҙ `AccountLoader<T>` лҘј ліҙл©ҙ вҖңzero-copyлқјм„ң л№ лҘҙм§Җл§Ң Anchor accountмҷҖ л№„мҠ·н•ҳкІҢ м•Ҳм „н•ҳлӢӨвҖқлҠ” мқёмғҒмқ„ л°ӣлҠ”лӢӨ. к·ёлҹ°лҚ° мқҙлІҲ upstream changeлҠ” initialized account pathм—җм„ңлҸ„ **owner/discriminator кІҖмҰқмқ„ мҡ°нҡҢн•ҳлҠ” кіөмӢқ м§„мһ…м җ** мқ„ лҚ” л…ём¶ңн–ҲлӢӨ. мқҙ мҲңк°„ мң„н—ҳмқҖ лӢЁмҲңн•ң `unsafe` мӮ¬мҡ©мқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј, **н”„л Ҳмһ„мӣҢнҒ¬к°Җ н—Ҳмҡ©н•ң convenience APIк°Җ кІҖмҰқ мҷ„лЈҢ к°қмІҙмІҳлҹј ліҙмқҙкё° мӢңмһ‘н•ңлӢӨ** лҠ” лҚ° мһҲлӢӨ. лҰ¬л·°м–ҙк°Җ вҖңмқҙлҜё Anchor loader нғҖмһ…мқҙлӢҲк№Ң кҙңм°®лӢӨвҖқкі  м§ҖлӮҳк°Җл©ҙ, мӢӨм ңлЎңлҠ” arbitrary same-layout bytesлҘј privileged stateмІҳлҹј мқҪкұ°лӮҳ м“°лҠ” trust collapseк°Җ мғқкёҙлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol introduces a zero-copy account path for hot state (`vault`, `config`, `oracle cache`, `strategy state`, `position sidecar`, etc.).
2. developer reaches for `AccountLoader::new_unchecked` or a similar owner/discriminator-bypass helper because the account is вҖңalready constrained elsewhereвҖқ, is part of a migration path, or is sourced from CPI / sidecar plumbing.
3. one call-site later, code performs `load()` / `load_mut()` and treats the returned struct as trusted initialized state.
4. attacker supplies an account that matches expected byte layout or passes a weaker outer constraint, but is not actually the intended typed account instance.
5. business logic now runs on forged/stale/aliased state: authority fields, limits, debt, weights, oracle metadata, or replay guards are read from attacker-controlled bytes.
6. result can be state confusion, policy bypass, stale-state replay, or mutation of the wrong logical object without any explicit signature failure.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **A96** лҠ” duplicate mutable aliasingмқҙлӢӨ. **A122** лҠ” aliasingмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **typed-state validation мһҗмІҙлҘј framework-level opt-outмңјлЎң л¬ҙл„ҲлңЁлҰ¬лҠ” нҢЁн„ҙ** мқҙлӢӨ.
- **A112** лҠ” raw IDL / metadata trust-boundary confusionмқҙлӢӨ. **A122** лҠ” IDLмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **runtime account loading path** мқҳ owner/discriminator invariantsк°Җ л¬ҙл„Ҳм§ҖлҠ” л¬ём ңлӢӨ.
- кё°мЎҙ generic вҖңaccount owner/discriminator check лҲ„лқҪвҖқ лғ„мғҲмҷҖлҸ„ лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ. **A122мқҳ мғҲ мҡ”мҶҢлҠ” кіөмӢқ zero-copy loader APIк°Җ opt-outмқ„ м •лӢ№нҷ”н•ҳл©ҙм„ң мҪ”л“ңлҰ¬л·° кІҪкі„к°Җ м•Ҫн•ҙм§„лӢӨ** лҠ” м җмқҙлӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. `AccountLoader<T>` мһҗмІҙк°Җ typed wrapperлқјм„ң raw `UncheckedAccount` ліҙлӢӨ нӣЁм”¬ м•Ҳм „н•ҙ ліҙмқёлӢӨ.
2. migration / CPI / plugin sidecar / performance optimization л¬ёл§Ҙм—җм„ң вҖңм—¬кё°м„ңлҠ” мқҙлҜё кІҖмҰқн–ҲлӢӨвҖқ лҠ” мЈјм„қмқҙ мүҪкІҢ н•©лҰ¬нҷ”лҗңлӢӨ.
3. zero-copy мҪ”л“ңлҠ” bytes вҶ” struct кІҪкі„к°Җ м§§м•„ ліҙм—¬лҸ„, мӢӨм ң trust boundaryлҠ” **лҲ„к°Җ owner/discriminatorлҘј ліҙмҰқн–ҲлҠ”к°Җ** м—җ лӢ¬л Ө мһҲлӢӨ.
4. framework upstream changeк°Җ feature нҳ•нғңлЎң л“Өм–ҙмҳӨл©ҙ, нҢҖмқҖ к·ёкІғмқ„ ліҙм•Ҳ мҷ„нҷ”к°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј м •мғҒ нҷ•мһҘмңјлЎң л°ӣм•„л“Өмқҙкё° мүҪлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: initialized account path uses unchecked zero-copy loader
// and later treats the bytes as fully validated state.
let loader = AccountLoader::<VaultState>::new_unchecked(vault_info);
let mut vault = loader.load_mut()?;
require!(vault.status == STATUS_ACTIVE, ErrorCode::VaultPaused);
vault.total_debt = vault.total_debt.checked_add(delta).unwrap();

// SAFER SHAPE: initialized state must keep owner + discriminator binding.
let loader = AccountLoader::<VaultState>::try_from(vault_info)?;
let mut vault = loader.load_mut()?;

// If an unchecked path is truly unavoidable, constrain it to creation-only
// or one-shot migration code and re-establish explicit owner/PDA/type checks.
require_keys_eq!(*vault_info.owner, program_id, ErrorCode::InvalidOwner);
require_keys_eq!(vault_info.key(), expected_pda, ErrorCode::InvalidPda);
assert_disc(vault_info, VaultState::DISCRIMINATOR)?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- initialized zero-copy stateм—җм„ңлҠ” `AccountLoader::try_from` лҘј кё°ліёк°’мңјлЎң кі м •н•ҳкі , `new_unchecked` / `try_from_unchecked` лҠ” **creation-only or one-shot migration** кІҪлЎңлЎң н•ңм •н•  кІғ
- unchecked loader мӮ¬мҡ© мӢң owner / PDA / discriminator / version fieldлҘј к°ҷмқҖ н•ЁмҲҳ м•Ҳм—җм„ң мһ¬кІҖмҰқн•ҳкі , к·ё кІҖмҰқмқҙ лҒқлӮҳкё° м „м—җлҠ” business fieldлҘј мқҪм§Җ л§җ кІғ
- code reviewм—җм„ң вҖңtyped wrapperлӢҲк№Ң м•Ҳм „вҖқ м¶”лЎ мқ„ кёҲм§Җн•ҳкі , zero-copy loader call-siteл§ҲлӢӨ **who established owner+type invariants?** лҘј мЈјм„қмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј н…ҢмҠӨнҠёлЎң мҰқлӘ…н•  кІғ
- fuzz / property testм—җ same-layout forged account, stale migrated account, wrong discriminator with valid size, wrong owner with valid bytes мјҖмқҙмҠӨлҘј м¶”к°Җн•  кІғ
- performance мөңм Ғнҷ”лӮҳ migration нҺёмқҳ л•Ңл¬ём—җ unchecked pathлҘј мғҒмӢң hot pathлЎң мҠ№кІ©н•ҳм§Җ л§җ кІғ

**Sources**: https://github.com/solana-foundation/anchor/commit/9d452e3aa450590bafd1e16590952ec64c9d8df7 | https://github.com/solana-foundation/anchor/pull/4162

**Microstable relevance**:
- нҳ„мһ¬ `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` мҷҖ `keeper/src/` мҠӨмә”м—җм„ң `AccountLoader`, `#[account(zero_copy)]`, `bytemuck`, `new_unchecked`, `try_from_unchecked` мӮ¬мҡ© нқ”м ҒмқҖ нҷ•мқёлҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ.
- мҳЁмІҙмқё кІҪлЎңмқҳ `UncheckedAccount` мӮ¬мҡ©мІҳ(`MigrateLegacyState`, `UpdateOraclePyth`, `DevnetForceReinit`)лҠ” zero-copy loaderк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј **мҲҳлҸҷ owner/discriminator/PDA кІҖмҰқ** мңјлЎң л°©м–ҙн•ҳкі  мһҲлӢӨ.
- keeper мёЎлҸ„ `wire::decode_account()` мҷҖ `oracle.rs` м—җм„ң discriminator/ownerлҘј мҲҳлҸҷ кІҖмҰқн•ңлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ keeper hot path лҳҗлҠ” on-chain state migrationмқ„ zero-copy convenience pathлЎң л°”кҫёл©ҙ A122лҠ” мҰүмӢң relevant н•ҙм§„лӢӨ. нҠ№нһҲ м§ҖкёҲмІҳлҹј Anchor `0.31.1` мқ„ м“°лҠ” мҪ”л“ңлІ мқҙмҠӨк°Җ н–Ҙнӣ„ upstream APIлҘј нқЎмҲҳн•  л•Ң вҖңм„ұлҠҘ мөңм Ғнҷ” кІё refactorвҖқ лЎң л“Өм–ҙмҳӨл©ҙ review missк°Җ лӮҳкё° мүҪлӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A122 Anchor Zero-Copy Validation Opt-Out / AccountLoader Trust Collapse | framework-supported unchecked zero-copy loader is used on initialized state, collapsing owner/discriminator/type invariants and turning typed-state reads into attacker-controlled or stale-byte interpretation | state confusion, forged authority/limit reads, stale-state replay, mutation of unintended logical object, policy bypass without explicit signer failure | current Microstable repo shows no `AccountLoader`/zero-copy usage and keeps manual owner/discriminator checks on raw-account paths; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future zero-copy refactor should treat unchecked loaders as a first-class red flag |

**Matrix state as of 2026-05-14 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A122** added after Anchor upstream formally exposed an owner/discriminator validation opt-out for zero-copy loaders. Matrix is now **129+ named vectors + META-01~63 + B73~B78 = 192+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A122 itself**; result is future-facing unless the codebase adopts zero-copy loaders.

## 2026-04-28 Multi-Round Bundle Simulation Pattern Addition

### D54. Multi-Round Transaction Simulation Dependency-Bomb / Bundle-Service Asymmetric DoS

**Source signals (2026-04-28 sweep)**:
- arXiv `2604.21169` (submitted 2026-04-23), *Position Paper: Denial-of-Service Against Multi-Round Transaction Simulation*

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ block builder / private relay / bundle endpointлҘј лӢЁмҲңн•ң "л№ лҘё мӮ¬м„Ө mempool" м •лҸ„лЎң м·Ёкёүн•ңлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң bundling serviceлҠ” ліҙнҶө **м—¬лҹ¬ transactionмқ„ мҲңм°Ё мӢңл®¬л Ҳмқҙм…ҳн•ҳл©ҙм„ң мқҙм „ txк°Җ л§Ңл“  stateлҘј лӢӨмқҢ txм—җ л°ҳмҳҒн•ҳлҠ” multi-round execution engine** мқҙлӢӨ. мқҙл•Ң кіөкІ©мһҗлҠ” мӢӨм ң мһҗліё мң„н—ҳ м—ҶмқҙлҸ„ **state dependencyлҘј кі мқҳлЎң м–ҪмқҖ bundle** мқ„ лҚҳм ё simulatorк°Җ round-by-roundлЎң л№„мӢј кі„мӮ°мқ„ л°ҳліөн•ҳкІҢ л§Ңл“Ө мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. н•өмӢ¬мқҖ л„ӨнҠёмӣҢнҒ¬ spamмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј, **bundle semantics мһҗмІҙлҘј мқҙмҡ©н•ң off-chain simulation-cost asymmetry** лӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. attacker identifies a builder / relay / bundle API that simulates candidate bundles before inclusion.
2. attacker crafts a bundle whose later transactions depend on storage/state mutated by earlier transactions, forcing full sequential simulation rather than cheap stateless validation.
3. bundle is designed so failure appears late, or succeeds only under narrow state combinations, keeping simulator work high while attacker capital at risk stays low.
4. atomic inclusion guarantees and bundle-specific admission rules are abused to avoid ordinary capital or mempool exposure costs.
5. repeated submissions consume builder simulation capacity, degrade searcher QoS, reduce builder revenue, or slow block production / bundle servicing.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D20 Denial of Service** лҠ” лҢҖк°ң л„ӨнҠёмӣҢнҒ¬ flood, tx spam, validator/path saturation к°ҷмқҖ broad availability attackмқ„ лӢӨлЈ¬лӢӨ. **D54** лҠ” **stateful multi-round bundle simulator** лқјлҠ” лҚ” мўҒкі  мғҲлЎңмҡҙ off-chain execution surfaceлҘј кІЁлғҘн•ңлӢӨ.
- **C25 MEV Extraction / A91 / A92** лҠ” orderingмқ„ мқҙмҡ©н•ҙ profitмқ„ лҪ‘лҠ” кіөкІ©мқҙлӢӨ. **D54** лҠ” ordering edgeк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј **admission/simulation cost curve мһҗмІҙ** лҘј кіөкІ©н•ңлӢӨ.
- н•өмӢ¬мқҖ chain congestionмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **builder-side sequential simulation semantics** лӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. мҳЁмІҙмқё к°җмӮ¬лҠ” contract correctnessм—җ м§‘мӨ‘н•ҳкі , bundle simulator / block-builder pipelineмқҖ infra лҳҗлҠ” performance concernмңјлЎң л¶„лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ.
2. л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ private orderflowлҘј anti-MEV defenseлЎң лҸ„мһ…н•ҳл©ҙм„ң, к·ё кІҪлЎңмқҳ **admission fairness / simulation budget** мқҖ лі„лҸ„ мң„нҳ‘лӘЁлҚёл§Ғн•ҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
3. лІҲл“Ө м„ңл№„мҠӨлҠ” кіөк°ң mempoolліҙлӢӨ нҸҗмҮ„м Ғмқҙлқј мһ¬нҳ„В·кҙҖмёЎмқҙ м–ҙл өкі , кіөкІ© cost modelлҸ„ мқјл°ҳ gas economicsмҷҖ лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ.
4. simulatorлҠ” correctness н…ҢмҠӨнҠёлҠ” мһҲм–ҙлҸ„ **adversarial cost asymmetry** н…ҢмҠӨнҠёк°Җ кұ°мқҳ м—ҶлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: bundle service simulates an attacker-controlled sequence
// with full state carry-over and no strict per-origin simulation budget.
fn evaluate_bundle(bundle: &[Tx]) -> SimResult {
    let mut state = load_head_state();
    for tx in bundle {
        state = simulate_with_state_carry(state, tx)?;
    }
    Ok(commit_candidate(state))
}

// SAFER SHAPE: cap multi-round depth/cost and reject dependency-heavy bundles
// before full sequential execution becomes attacker-amplifiable.
fn evaluate_bundle(bundle: &[Tx], budget: &mut Budget) -> SimResult {
    enforce_bundle_size_limit(bundle)?;
    reject_excessive_state_dependency(bundle)?;
    budget.charge_precheck(bundle)?;
    ...
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- bundle / private-relay pathм—җ **per-origin simulation budget, round cap, dependency-depth cap** мқ„ л‘ҳ кІғ
- мӢӨнҢЁк°Җ лҠҰкІҢ л°ңмғқн• мҲҳлЎқ л№„мҡ©мқҙ м»Өм§ҖлҠ” late-fail bundleмқ„ лі„лҸ„ м җмҲҳнҷ”н•  кІғ
- "private orderflow = safer" к°Җм •кіј лі„к°ңлЎң **simulator-plane DoS** лҘј лҸ…лҰҪ н•ӯлӘ©мңјлЎң мң„нҳ‘лӘЁлҚёл§Ғн•  кІғ
- precheck лӢЁкі„м—җм„ң state-carry dependencyк°Җ кіјлҸ„н•ң bundleмқ„ мЎ°кё° кұ°м Ҳн•ҳкі , admission costлҘј attackerм—җкІҢ м „к°Җн•  мһҘм№ҳлҘј л‘ҳ кІғ
- public RPC fallbackмқҙ мһҲлҚ”лқјлҸ„ bundle endpoint overload мӢң мһҗлҸҷ fail-openмңјлЎң лҚ” м•Ҫн•ң кІҪлЎңм—җ л–Ём–ҙм§Җм§Җ м•ҠкІҢ н•  кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- нҳ„мһ¬ `programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` л°Ҹ `keeper/src/` мҠӨмә”м—җм„ң `Jito`, `bundle`, `sendBundle`, `dontfront`, private relay, local multi-round bundle simulator нқ”м ҒмқҖ нҷ•мқёлҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ keeper rebalances / oracle writes / hedge legsлҘј **Jito bundle лҳҗлҠ” private relay** лЎң ліҙлӮҙкё° мӢңмһ‘н•ҳл©ҙ D54лҠ” мҰүмӢң мһ¬нҸүк°Җн•ҙм•ј н•ңлӢӨ. нҠ№нһҲ anti-MEV лӘ©м ҒмңјлЎң private orderflowлҘј л¶ҷмқҙлҠ” мҲңк°„, **MEV л°©м–ҙ кІҪлЎңк°Җ мғҲ DoS control plane** мңјлЎң л°”лҖ” мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D54 Multi-Round Transaction Simulation Dependency-Bomb / Bundle-Service Asymmetric DoS | attacker submits state-dependent bundles that force expensive sequential simulator work while keeping own capital/risk low, degrading bundle-service throughput and builder revenue | private-relay slowdown, searcher QoS degradation, builder revenue loss, delayed bundle execution, fail-open risk into weaker public path | current Microstable repo shows **no Jito/private bundle path today**, so **NOT ACTIVE**; future bundle-based keeper routing must treat simulation-budget abuse as a first-class security invariant |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-28 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D54** added after the new arXiv work made **bundle-service simulation-cost asymmetry** concrete enough to separate from generic DoS. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from D54 itself**; the open **B45 audit-attestation gap** remains the top current HIGH continuity issue.

## 2026-05-06 DNSSEC Closest-Encloser Root-Stall Validation-Loop Pattern Addition

### D55. DNSSEC Closest-Encloser Root-Stall Loop / Cross-Zone Validation OOM

**Source signals (2026-05-06 sweep)**:
- RustSec `RUSTSEC-2026-0118` (`hickory-proto`) and `RUSTSEC-2026-0120` (`hickory-net`) вҖ” issued 2026-05-01

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ DNSSEC validatorлҘј л¶ҷмқҙл©ҙ "cache poisoningмқҖ мӨ„кі  м•Ҳм „н•ҙм§„лӢӨ" кі  мғқк°Ғн•ңлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң мқҙлІҲ мӢ нҳёмқҳ н•өмӢ¬мқҖ **кІҖмҰқмқ„ лҚ” м—„кІ©н•ҳкІҢ н•ң кІҪлЎң мһҗмІҙк°Җ мғҲлЎңмҡҙ liveness kill-switch** к°Җ лҗ  мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨлҠ” м җмқҙлӢӨ. `QNAME` мқҙ `SOA owner` мқҳ мһҗмҶҗмқј кІғмқҙлқјлҠ” к°Җм •мқҙ к№Ём§„ cross-zone мқ‘лӢө н•ҳлӮҳл§ҢмңјлЎң, closest-encloser proof кІҖмҰқ лЈЁн”„к°Җ **DNS rootм—җм„ң л©Ҳм¶ҳ мұ„ кі„мҶҚ `base_name()` кіј hash candidate allocationмқ„ л°ҳліө** н•ңлӢӨ. мҰү л¬ём ңлҠ” лӢЁмҲң parser panicмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј, **proof-validation state machineмқҙ trust-boundary мң„л°ҳмқ„ мў…лЈҢ мЎ°кұҙ м—Ҷмқҙ нқЎмҲҳ** н•ңлӢӨлҠ” лҚ° мһҲлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. victim keeper / bridge watcher / oracle fetcher uses a Hickory DNSSEC-validating path (`DnssecDnsHandle`) for endpoint resolution.
2. attacker can return or influence a NoData / NXDomain response requiring NSEC3 closest-encloser proof, often via insecure CNAME crossing into a DNSSEC-signed zone.
3. authority section carries an SOA whose owner is **not an ancestor** of the queried name.
4. validator walks `QNAME -> parent -> ... -> root`, but the termination condition is `current == soa_name`; because `soa_name` is never reached, the loop stalls at root.
5. debug builds panic at the root assertion; release builds allocate candidate names / hashes unbounded until memory exhaustion, stalling or killing resolution for downstream RPC / oracle traffic.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D20** = generic resource-exhaustion DoS.
- **D55** = a **DNSSEC proof-validation loop** whose stopping condition silently assumes zone ancestry and therefore becomes attacker-triggerable through cross-zone response semantics.
- **D53** = resolver cache-poisoning via wrong authority-binding context.
- **D55** = no cache poisoning is required; the validator dies while trying to prove non-existence.
- **B58** = QUIC transport-layer panic / liveness loss after name resolution.
- **D55** = resolution itself becomes the kill plane before transport is even reached.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. DNSSEC validation pathлҠ” "ліҙм•Ҳ к°•нҷ” мҳөм…ҳ" мңјлЎң ліҙмқҙкё° мү¬мӣҢ availability threat-modelмқ„ лі„лҸ„лЎң м„ёмҡ°м§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
2. loop bodyк°Җ `base_name()` progressionмқ„ н•ҳлҜҖлЎң superficially terminatingн•ҙ ліҙмқҙм§Җл§Ң, root лҸ„лӢ¬ нӣ„ invariantк°Җ к№Ём§Ҳ л•Ң release buildм—җм„ңлҠ” кі„мҶҚ н• лӢ№л§Ң л°ҳліөн•ңлӢӨ.
3. resolver / recursor / clientк°Җ к°ҷмқҖ `DnssecDnsHandle` primitiveлҘј кіөмң н•ҙ blast radiusк°Җ л„“мқҖлҚ°, лҰ¬л·°лҠ” нҠ№м • binary лӢЁмң„лЎңл§Ң мқҙлӨ„м§Җкё° мүҪлӢӨ.
4. мҡҙмҳҒмһҗлҠ” DNS мһҘм• лҘј upstream outageлЎң мҳӨм§„н•ҳкё° мүҪкі , root causeк°Җ cross-zone NSEC3 validation pathлқјлҠ” м җмқ„ лҠҰкІҢ ліёлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: loop terminates only when current candidate == soa owner,
// implicitly assuming soa_name is an ancestor of qname.
let mut name = qname.clone();
while name != soa_name {
    candidates.push(name.clone());
    hashed.push(hash_name(&name)?);
    name = name.base_name(); // stalls at root if soa_name is not an ancestor
}

// SAFER SHAPE: explicitly break on root / non-ancestor condition before
// allocating more candidates.
let mut name = qname.clone();
loop {
    if name == soa_name {
        break;
    }
    if name.is_root() || !qname.zone_of(&soa_name) {
        return Err(Error::InvalidCrossZoneClosestEncloser);
    }
    candidates.push(name.clone());
    hashed.push(hash_name(&name)?);
    name = name.base_name();
}
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- `hickory-net >= 0.26.1` лҳҗлҠ” лҸҷл“ұ нҢЁм№ҳ кІҪлЎңлЎң мқҙн–үн•  кІғ
- closest-encloser candidate walkм—җ **root break / ancestor proof / allocation cap** мқ„ лҸҷмӢңм—җ л‘ҳ кІғ
- DNSSEC validationмқ„ мјңлҠ” кІҪмҡ° insecure CNAME вҶ’ signed-zone NoData/NXDomain көҗм°Ё кІҪлЎңлҘј regression testм—җ л„Јмқ„ кІғ
- security-critical RPC/oracle hostname resolutionм—җм„ң DNSSEC validator failureлҘј лӢЁмҲң retry loopлЎң л¬»м§Җ л§җкі  circuit-breaker + fallback policyлЎң л¶„лҰ¬н•  кІғ
- local validating resolver / sidecarлҘј л¶ҷмқј л•ҢлҠ” "validation strictness мҰқк°Җ = availability risk мҰқк°Җ" лҘј лі„лҸ„ SLOлЎң лӘЁлҚёл§Ғн•  кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/Cargo.lock`, `keeper/Cargo.toml`, `keeper/src/price_feed.rs` мҠӨмә”м—җм„ң `hickory`, `hickory-net`, `hickory-proto`, `trust-dns`, custom DNSSEC validator кІҪлЎңлҠ” нҷ•мқёлҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ.
- нҳ„мһ¬ keeperлҠ” `reqwest`, `solana-client`, мӢңмҠӨн…ң DNS н•ҙм„қ кІҪлЎңлҘј мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳл©°, л ҲнҸ¬ лӮҙ local DNSSEC-validating resolver / recursor нқ”м ҒлҸ„ ліҙмқҙм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ RPC/oracle failover м•һлӢЁм—җ Rust-native validating resolverлӮҳ DNSSEC sidecarлҘј л¶ҷмқҙл©ҙ D55лҠ” мҰүмӢң relevant н•ҙм§„лӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D55 DNSSEC Closest-Encloser Root-Stall Loop / Cross-Zone Validation OOM | DNSSEC validator assumes SOA owner is an ancestor of QNAME during closest-encloser proof construction; cross-zone response stalls at root and allocates unbounded candidates until panic/OOM | endpoint-resolution halt, keeper/oracle liveness loss, debug panic or release OOM, possible fail-open into weaker resolver path | current Microstable dependency scan found no Hickory / DNSSEC validator path, so **NOT ACTIVE today**; future local validating resolver adoption must treat validation-loop availability as a first-class trust boundary |

**Matrix state as of 2026-05-06 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D55** added after classifying `RUSTSEC-2026-0118` / `0120` as a **DNSSEC validation-state-machine liveness failure** distinct from D53 resolver cache poisoning and generic D20 resource DoS. Matrix is now **129+ named vectors + META-01~65 + B73~B78 = 194+ total entries**. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from D55 itself**; current result is future-facing unless a validating resolver layer is introduced.

## 2026-04-26 Recursive DNS Resolver Cache-Poisoning Pattern Addition

### D53. Recursive DNS Sibling-Zone NS Cache Poisoning / Parent-Pool Zone-Context Elevation

**Source signals (2026-04-26 sweep)**:
- RustSec `RUSTSEC-2026-0106` / `GHSA-83hf-93m4-rgwq` (issued 2026-04-22)

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ нҢҖмқҙ keeper / relayer / price fetcher / verifierм—җм„ң `https://allowlisted-host` л§Ң к°•м ңн•ҳл©ҙ DNS лӢЁкі„лҠ” мқҙлҜё лҒқлӮң л¬ём ңлқјкі  ліёлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң мқҙлІҲ мӢ нҳёлҠ” лӢӨлҘҙлӢӨ. **м·Ём•Ҫн•ң recursive resolverк°Җ sibling zoneмқҳ AUTHORITY м„№м…ҳ NS л ҲмҪ”л“ңлҘј parent-zone contextлЎң нҶөкіјмӢңнӮӨл©ҙ, кіөкІ©мһҗлҠ” л„ӨнҠёмӣҢнҒ¬ кІҪлЎңлҘј нғҲм·Ён•ҳм§Җ м•Ҡкі лҸ„ лӢӨлҘё allowlisted zoneмқҳ authoritative NS cacheлҘј мҳӨм—ј** мӢңнӮ¬ мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ. мҰү кіөкІ©л©ҙмқҖ DNS/BGP hijackмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **resolver лӮҙл¶Җмқҳ query-to-cache trust binding** мқҙлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. victim keeper / bot / gateway uses a vulnerable Hickory recursive resolver path for outbound hostname resolution.
2. attacker controls or can answer for a sibling child zone under the same parent delegation boundary.
3. attacker returns an AUTHORITY-section NS record for `victim.poc.` while servicing a lookup for `attacker.poc.`.
4. resolver validates the record against the parent pool zone (`poc.`) instead of the originally queried child zone, so the bailiwick-style check passes and caches `(victim.poc., NS) -> ns.evil.poc.`.
5. subsequent lookups for the victim zone are routed to attacker-controlled authority, enabling false endpoint resolution, service-discovery poisoning, or staged pairing with TLS / verifier weaknesses.

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **D27** = DNS/BGP or provider-level endpoint takeover that redirects traffic on the network path.
- **D53** = the victim can keep the same hostname, same config, and same upstream allowlist, yet a **recursive resolver cache entry** is poisoned from a sibling-zone response path.
- **A115 / A77** = certificate / revocation verification failures after resolution.
- **D53** = the earlier **authority-discovery cache** is corrupted first, and may later be paired with TLS bugs or misissuance.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. custom recursive resolverлҠ” performance / availability componentлЎңл§Ң ліҙмқҙкі , authority-binding boundaryлЎң мһҳ лӘЁлҚёл§Ғлҗҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
2. bailiwick-style checkк°Җ мҪ”л“ңм—җ мЎҙмһ¬н•ҳл©ҙ, zone contextк°Җ мһҳлӘ»лҗң мұ„ м Ғмҡ©лҸјлҸ„ superficially safeн•ҙ ліҙмқёлӢӨ.
3. hostname allowlist, TLS, and RPC quorum reviewк°Җ ліҙнҶө лҚ” лҲҲм—җ лқ„м–ҙ, resolver cache key semanticsлҠ” к№ҠкІҢ ліҙм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
4. exploitмқҖ registrar takeoverлӮҳ obvious config diff м—Ҷмқҙ мқјм–ҙлӮҳлҜҖлЎң incident triageк°Җ upstream DNS compromiseлЎңл§Ң мўҒнҳҖм§Җкё° мүҪлӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: cache keys records by their own owner name/type and
// evaluates AUTHORITY records against the parent NS-pool zone, not the
// originating query tuple.
for rr in answer.iter().chain(authority.iter()).chain(additional.iter()) {
    if rr.name().is_subzone(ns_pool.zone()) {
        cache.insert((rr.name().clone(), rr.record_type()), rr.clone());
    }
}

// SAFER SHAPE: cache the full response under the originating query, or enforce
// that AUTHORITY data can only populate cache entries scoped to the query zone.
cache.insert((query.name().clone(), query.record_type()), response.clone());
validate_authority_scope(&response, query.name())?;
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- `hickory-recursor` лҘј productionм—җ м“°м§Җ л§җкі , `hickory-resolver >= 0.26.0` мқҳ response-keyed recursor path лҳҗлҠ” лҸҷл“ұн•ң мҲҳм • кІҪлЎңлЎң мқҙн–үн•  кІғ
- security-sensitive RPC / oracle / attestation hostnameм—җ лҢҖн•ҙ **authoritative NS set drift** лҘј лі„лҸ„ к°җмӢңн•  кІғ
- local recursive resolverлҘј м“ё л•Ң sibling-zone poisoning regression testлҘј л„Јмқ„ кІғ
- hostname allowlistлҘј DNS trust boundary м „мІҙмқҳ лҢҖмІҙмһ¬лЎң м·Ёкёүн•ҳм§Җ л§җ кІғ
- failover / multi-RPC м„Өкі„м—җм„ңлҸ„ resolver monocultureлҘј н”јн•ҳкі  independent resolution planeмқ„ л‘ҳ кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- `microstable/solana/Cargo.lock` мҷҖ keeper мқҳмЎҙм„ұ мҠӨмә”м—җм„ң `hickory`, `hickory-recursor`, `trust-dns` лҠ” нҷ•мқёлҗҳм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ.
- нҳ„мһ¬ keeperлҠ” `reqwest`, `solana-client`, мӢңмҠӨн…ң н•ҙм„қ кІҪлЎңлҘј м“°л©°, л ҲнҸ¬ лӮҙ custom recursive resolver / DNS sidecar кө¬м„ұ нқ”м ҒлҸ„ ліҙмқҙм§Җ м•Ҡм•ҳлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today**.
- лӢӨл§Ң н–Ҙнӣ„ лЎңм»¬ recursor, service-discovery sidecar, Rust-native DNS resolution layerлҘј RPC/oracle failover м•һлӢЁм—җ лҸ„мһ…н•ҳл©ҙ D53мқҖ мҰүмӢң relevant н•ҙм§„лӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| D53 Recursive DNS Sibling-Zone NS Cache Poisoning / Parent-Pool Zone-Context Elevation | vulnerable recursive resolver caches AUTHORITY-section NS records for a sibling zone under parent-pool context, poisoning authoritative delegation for later lookups | false endpoint resolution, service-discovery poisoning, allowlisted-host trust bypass precursor, potential pairing with TLS/verifier bugs | current Microstable dependency scan found no `hickory` / `trust-dns` path and no custom recursive resolver; **NOT ACTIVE today**, but any future local recursor or DNS sidecar would make this immediately relevant |

**Matrix state as of 2026-04-26 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **D53** added after classifying `RUSTSEC-2026-0106` as a resolver-cache authority-binding bug distinct from D27 routing takeover and A115 certificate validation failures. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM active finding** in this cycle; the new pattern is future-facing unless a custom recursive resolver layer is introduced.

## 2026-07-09 Async Vault Share-Mint Lifecycle Pattern Addition

### A137. External Share-Mint Lifecycle Authority / Async Claim-Path Brick

**Source signals (2026-07-09 sweep)**:
- Solana Foundation `vault` README (current public repo surface, observed in the recent 7-day window)

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ Solana нҢҖмқҙ `bring-your-own share mint` лҘј upgrade нҡҢн”јмҡ© мң м—°м„ұмңјлЎңл§Ң ліёлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң async deposit/redemption vaultм—җм„ңлҠ” share mintк°Җ лӢЁмҲң token metadataк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј **queued claim/refund/redeem settlementлҘј мқҙм–ҙ мЈјлҠ” lifecycle authority object** лӢӨ. мҷёл¶Җ share mintм—җ `MintCloseAuthority`, `freeze authority`, `default-frozen` к°ҷмқҖ Token-2022 к¶Ңн•ңмқҙ лӮЁм•„ мһҲмңјл©ҙ, attackerлҠ” vault coreлҘј к№Ём§Җ м•Ҡкі лҸ„ **claim path мһҗмІҙлҘј мҳҒкө¬ лҳҗлҠ” мһҘкё°м ҒмңјлЎң лёҢлҰӯ** мӢңнӮ¬ мҲҳ мһҲлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. protocol accepts a pre-configured external share mint and settles deposits/redemptions asynchronously rather than atomically.
2. users submit deposit or redeem requests that land in a queue and must later mint, burn, or transfer shares/assets to complete.
3. attacker compromises or legitimately controls a residual mint lifecycle authority that the vault did not neutralize (`MintCloseAuthority`, `freeze authority`, `default-frozen`, or equivalent external authority on the share/asset side).
4. attacker closes the share mint when supply temporarily reaches zero, or freezes `reserve` / `pending_vault` style token accounts before queued settlement executes.
5. queued refund / claim / redeem paths can no longer mint, burn, or transfer the required token, so the protocol enters manual-recovery mode or selective liveness collapse even though the core vault code and accounting state remain вҖңcorrect.вҖқ

**Why this is distinct from existing vectors**:
- **B44** лҠ” active delegate / `PermanentDelegate` лЎң **token movement authority** к°Җ лӮЁм•„ direct drainмқҙ к°ҖлҠҘн•ң нҢЁн„ҙмқҙлӢӨ. **A137** мқҖ delegate drainмқҙ м•„лӢҲлқј **share-mint lifecycle authorityк°Җ async settlement livenessлҘј мқём§ҲлЎң мһЎлҠ” нҢЁн„ҙ** мқҙлӢӨ.
- **A6** лҠ” wrong account acceptanceлӢӨ. **A137** мқҖ кі„м •мқҙ л§һм•„лҸ„, к·ё кі„м •м—җ лӮЁм•„ мһҲлҠ” **external lifecycle authority** л•Ңл¬ём—җ queue completionмқҙ л¬ҙл„Ҳм§„лӢӨ.
- **A10** к°ҷмқҖ generic logic bugлЎң лӯүк°ңл©ҙ мӨ‘мҡ”н•ң көҗнӣҲмқ„ лҶ“м№ңлӢӨ. н•өмӢ¬мқҖ vault code мһҗмІҙк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј **external mint lifecycle ownershipмқҙ business completion semanticsлҘј мўҢмҡ°н•ңлӢӨ** лҠ” м җмқҙлӢӨ.

**мҷң к°җмӮ¬к°Җ лҶ“м№ҳлҠ”к°Җ**:
1. share mintлҘј вҖңмқҙлҜё мЎҙмһ¬н•ҳлҠ” мҷёл¶Җ мһҗмӮ°вҖқ мңјлЎң м·Ёкёүн•ҙ vault scope л°–мңјлЎң л°Җм–ҙлӮҙкё° мүҪлӢӨ.
2. sync vaultм—җм„ңлҠ” freeze/close authorityк°Җ nuisanceмІҳлҹј ліҙмқҙм§Җл§Ң, async queueм—җм„ңлҠ” **нӣ„н–ү settlement completion мЎ°кұҙ** мқҙ лҗңлӢӨ.
3. л§ҺмқҖ лҰ¬л·°к°Җ `mint authority moved to vault PDA` м—җм„ң л©Ҳм¶”кі , `MintCloseAuthority` / freeze / default-frozen к°ҷмқҖ **adjacent lifecycle authority** лҘј лҒқк№Ңм§Җ inventoryн•ҳм§Җ м•ҠлҠ”лӢӨ.
4. мӮ¬кі к°Җ лӮҳлҸ„ вҖңмҪ”л“ң лІ„к·ёк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј мҡҙмҳҒмғҒ mint м„Өм • л¬ём ңвҖқ лЎң м¶•мҶҢлҸј мһ¬л°ң л°©м§Җ мІҙнҒ¬лҰ¬мҠӨнҠём—җ мһҳ м•Ҳ лӮЁлҠ”лӢӨ.

**Code pattern to find**:
```rust
// VULNERABLE SHAPE: protocol accepts an external share mint and later assumes
// queued claims can always mint/burn/transfer against it.
pub fn initialize_vault(ctx: Context<InitializeVault>) -> Result<()> {
    vault.share_mint = ctx.accounts.share_mint.key();
    // MISSING: reject MintCloseAuthority / freeze authority / default-frozen
    // MISSING: prove lifecycle authorities are burned or protocol-owned
    Ok(())
}

// Later, async settlement assumes the mint still exists and is transferable.
pub fn settle_claim(ctx: Context<SettleClaim>) -> Result<()> {
    token_2022::mint_to(ctx.accounts.into_mint_ctx(), claim_amount)?;
    Ok(())
}

// SAFER SHAPE: external mint lifecycle authorities are neutralized or protocol-owned.
require!(share_mint.close_authority.is_none(), ErrorCode::ShareMintCloseAuthorityNotAllowed);
require!(share_mint.freeze_authority.is_none(), ErrorCode::ShareMintFreezeAuthorityNotAllowed);
require!(!share_mint.default_frozen, ErrorCode::ShareMintDefaultFrozenNotAllowed);
```

**Defensive heuristic**:
- async vault / queued settlement кө¬мЎ°м—җм„ңлҠ” `mint authority` лҝҗ м•„лӢҲлқј **`MintCloseAuthority`, freeze authority, default-frozen, external lifecycle controller` м „мІҙ** лҘј release gateлЎң inventoryн•  кІғ
- external share mintлҘј л°ӣлҚ”лқјлҸ„ **protocol-owned or burned authorities only** мӣҗм№ҷмқ„ кё°ліёк°’мңјлЎң л‘ҳ кІғ
- `queued request created` мҷҖ `queued request can still settle` лҘј лӢӨлҘё л¶ҲліҖмӢқмңјлЎң л¶„лҰ¬н•ҳкі , share mint lifecycle regression testлҘј м¶”к°Җн•  кІғ
- asset sideм—җм„ң `PermanentDelegate` к°ҷмқҖ direct-drain к¶Ңн•ңмқҖ **B44** лЎң, share/queue liveness-kill к¶Ңн•ңмқҖ **A137** лЎң л”°лЎң м¶”м Ғн•  кІғ
- zero-supply мҲңк°„м—җлҸ„ close authorityк°Җ future issuance laneмқ„ мҳҒкө¬ м°ЁлӢЁн•ҳм§Җ лӘ»н•ңлӢӨлҠ” negative testлҘј л‘ҳ кІғ

**Microstable relevance**:
- reviewed live path `microstable/solana/programs/microstable/src/lib.rs` лҠ” classic SPL Token via `anchor_spl::token` мқ„ мӮ¬мҡ©н•ҳкі , protocol-owned mint/vaultлҘј м§Ғм ‘ мҙҲкё°нҷ”н•ңлӢӨ.
- current codebaseм—җлҠ” external share mint onboarding, async deposit/redemption queue, `pending_vault`, Token-2022 mint extension pathк°Җ м—ҶлӢӨ.
- л”°лқјм„ң **NOT ACTIVE today** лӢӨ.
- лӢӨл§Ң future RWA / queued redemption / external collateral share-token integrationмқ„ л¶ҷмқҙл©ҙ A137мқҖ мҰүмӢң first-class review itemмқҙ лҗңлӢӨ.

| Vector | Mechanism | Impact | Microstable relevance |
|---|---|---|---|
| A137 External Share-Mint Lifecycle Authority / Async Claim-Path Brick | async vault relies on an externally supplied share mint whose residual close/freeze/default-frozen authority can later brick queued mint/burn/transfer settlement without breaking core vault accounting | queued refund/claim deadlock, selective liveness collapse, manual recovery, hidden dependence on adjacent mint authority surface | current Microstable live path has no external share mint or async queue, so **NOT ACTIVE today**; future queued-settlement / RWA vault features must inventory share-mint lifecycle authorities explicitly |

**Matrix state as of 2026-07-09 (red-team daily update)**: prior coverage retained; **A137** added to separate **async settlement liveness collapse through external share-mint lifecycle authority** from B44 delegate-drain and generic config/logic bugs. Matrix is now **147+ named vectors + META-01~71 + B73~B84 = 218+ total entries** before same-day D56. Microstable has **no new CRITICAL/HIGH active finding from A137 itself**; current result is future-facing unless async queued vault semantics are introduced.

## 2026-07-09 XML Parser Validation-Cost Ceiling Pattern Addition

### D56. XML Start-Tag Validation Core-Pin / Namespace Heap Balloon

**Source signals (2026-07-09 sweep)**:
- RustSec `RUSTSEC-2026-0194` (issued 2026-07-02)
- RustSec `RUSTSEC-2026-0195` (issued 2026-07-02)

**Key insight**: л§ҺмқҖ keeper / verifier / attestation consumerлҠ” parserлҘј лӢЁмҲң decode helperлЎң ліёлӢӨ. н•ҳм§Җл§Ң мқҙлІҲ мӢ нҳёмқҳ н•өмӢ¬мқҖ **validation path inside the parser** мһҗмІҙк°Җ attack surfaceлқјлҠ” м җмқҙлӢӨ. `quick-xml` мқҳ old duplicate-attribute checkлҠ” н•ҳлӮҳмқҳ huge start tagл§ҢмңјлЎң **pure CPU quadratic core pin** мқ„ л§Ңл“Өкі , `NsReader` namespace resolutionмқҖ consumerк°Җ eventлҘј ліҙкё° м „л¶Җн„° **мҲЁмқҖ heap balloon** мқ„ л§Ңл“Өм–ҙ OOMмқ„ мң лҸ„н•ңлӢӨ. мҰү л¬ём ңлҠ” malformed XML crashк°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј, **м •мғҒмІҳлҹј ліҙмқҙлҠ” start tag н•ҳлӮҳк°Җ assurance layerлҘј cost-amplification actuatorлЎң л°”кҫјлӢӨ** лҠ” лҚ° мһҲлӢӨ.

**Attack chain**:
1. victim control-plane service accepts untrusted XML for oracle metadata, attestation, RPKI-style proofs, validator manifests, or any adjacent ingest path.
2. attacker sends a single start tag with either (a) a huge number of distinct attributes or (b) a huge number of `xmlns` declarations.
3. parser performs duplicate-name validation with quadratic comparisons or pushes namespace bindings into internal buffers/Vec without an enforceable ceiling.
